sentences.zh: 现在，苍白意味着困在室内工作，晒黑意味着外出休闲。

sentences.en: This is suddenly changing, because “being healthy” means you have time to do this.In the media and art we consume, the amount of human effort involved drives how much we subconsciously value it.
sentences.zh: 这种情况突然发生了变化，因为“健康”意味着你有时间去做这件事。在我们消费的媒体和艺术中，所涉及的人类努力的数量决定了我们下意识地重视它的程度。

sentences.en: Subtle indicators of human involvement in writing, for example, will make us like the writing more.
sentences.zh: 例如，人类参与写作的微妙迹象会让我们更喜欢写作。

sentences.en: A video clearly made by a real human will be more entertaining.
sentences.zh: 由真人制作的清晰视频会更有趣。

sentences.en: Just like a meal cooked at home by some with passion for the art will always taste better than a chain restaurant meal, even if the restaurant meal is technically more complicated or uses better ingredients.As AI takes over more and more, its direct output and stuff that smells like its direct output is going to be less and less desirable.There is a TON of market opportunity in business for people who truly understand this.
sentences.zh: 就像一些对艺术充满热情的人在家做的饭菜总是比连锁餐厅的饭菜味道更好，即使餐厅的饭菜在技术上更复杂或者使用更好的食材。随着人工智能越来越多地接管，它的直接输出和闻起来像直接输出的东西将越来越不受欢迎。对于真正了解这一点的人来说，商业中有大量的市场机会。

blocks.html_en: The reason is that AI art is now commodified.

sentences.en: The reason is that AI art is now commodified.
sentences.zh: 原因是人工智能艺术现在已经商品化。

blocks.html_en: Beauty standards, for example, track directly alongside what a body looks like when the owner has free time to spend on leisure. For example, a tan in some cultures used to be ugly and pale skin more beautiful because a tan meant you worked outside in the sun and pale skin meant you could be inside, doing a higher status job or not working. Now, pale means stuck inside working and tan means going outside for leisure. This is suddenly changing, because “being healthy” means you have time to do this.

sentences.en: Beauty standards, for example, track directly alongside what a body looks like when the owner has free time to spend on leisure.
sentences.zh: 例如，当主人有空闲时间休闲时，美容标准直接与身体的外观相匹配。

sentences.en: For example, a tan in some cultures used to be ugly and pale skin more beautiful because a tan meant you worked outside in the sun and pale skin meant you could be inside, doing a higher status job or not working.
sentences.zh: 例如，在某些文化中，晒黑的皮肤过去是丑陋的，而苍白的皮肤更美丽，因为晒黑的皮肤意味着你在阳光下工作，而苍白的皮肤意味着你可以在室内，从事更高地位的工作或不工作。

sentences.en: Now, pale means stuck inside working and tan means going outside for leisure.
sentences.zh: 现在，苍白意味着困在室内工作，晒黑意味着外出休闲。

sentences.en: This is suddenly changing, because “being healthy” means you have time to do this.
sentences.zh: 这种情况突然发生了变化，因为“健康”意味着你有时间这样做。

blocks.html_en: In the media and art we consume, the amount of human effort involved drives how much we subconsciously value it. Subtle indicators of human involvement in writing, for example, will make us like the writing more. A video clearly made by a real human will be more entertaining. Just like a meal cooked at home by some with passion for the art will always taste better than a chain restaurant meal, even if the restaurant meal is technically more complicated or uses better ingredients.

sentences.en: In the media and art we consume, the amount of human effort involved drives how much we subconsciously value it.
sentences.zh: 在我们消费的媒体和艺术中，人类的努力程度决定了我们潜意识中对它的重视程度。

sentences.en: Subtle indicators of human involvement in writing, for example, will make us like the writing more.
sentences.zh: 例如，人类参与写作的微妙迹象会让我们更喜欢写作。

sentences.en: A video clearly made by a real human will be more entertaining.
sentences.zh: 由真人制作的清晰视频会更有趣。

sentences.en: Just like a meal cooked at home by some with passion for the art will always taste better than a chain restaurant meal, even if the restaurant meal is technically more complicated or uses better ingredients.
sentences.zh: 就像一些对艺术充满热情的人在家做的饭菜总是比连锁餐厅的饭菜味道更好，即使餐厅的饭菜在技术上更复杂或者使用更好的食材。

blocks.html_en: As AI takes over more and more, its direct output and stuff that smells like its direct output is going to be less and less desirable.

sentences.en: As AI takes over more and more, its direct output and stuff that smells like its direct output is going to be less and less desirable.
sentences.zh: 随着人工智能越来越多地接管，它的直接输出和类似直接输出的东西将越来越不受欢迎。

blocks.html_en: There is a TON of market opportunity in business for people who truly understand this.

sentences.en: There is a TON of market opportunity in business for people who truly understand this.
sentences.zh: 对于真正了解这一点的人来说，商业中有大量的市场机会。

blocks.html_en:
> The most heartbreaking ones I've seen are in schools, or anywhere that children are involved.

Not heartbreaking, but the ones that get my goat up most are flyers from craft fairs and worse places promising to teach crafts/arts. How is that not a sign to walk very quickly in the other direction? Or maybe it is intentional, like some bad spam text apparently being intentional to weed out the people who are less likely to fall from the scam anyway…

sentences.en: > The most heartbreaking ones I've seen are in schools, or anywhere that children are involved.Not heartbreaking, but the ones that get my goat up most are flyers from craft fairs and worse places promising to teach crafts/arts.
sentences.zh: > 我见过的最令人心碎的地方是在学校或任何有孩子参与的地方。并不令人心碎，但最让我兴奋的是来自工艺品博览会和更糟糕的地方的传单，承诺教授工艺品/艺术。

sentences.en: How is that not a sign to walk very quickly in the other direction?
sentences.zh: 这怎么不是一个要快速朝另一个方向走的标志呢？

sentences.en: Or maybe it is intentional, like some bad spam text apparently being intentional to weed out the people who are less likely to fall from the scam anyway…
sentences.zh: 或者它可能是故意的，就像一些不良垃圾邮件文本显然是故意清除那些不太可能陷入骗局的人......

blocks.html_en: My wife and I just doesn't a weekend at a craft show (she has a hobby writing sci fi books and we go to street fests to sell them). There were at least TWO vendors selling "hand made" paintings that I could tell were AI generated pictures pasted onto a piece of wood and then had a few coats of clear coat slapped on thick to give it a paint brush effect. Also, their banners were AI generated. Disgustingly dishonest.

sentences.en: My wife and I just doesn't a weekend at a craft show (she has a hobby writing sci fi books and we go to street fests to sell them).
sentences.zh: 我和我的妻子只是周末不去看工艺品展（她有写科幻书的爱好，我们去街头集会卖书）。

sentences.en: There were at least TWO vendors selling "hand made" paintings that I could tell were AI generated pictures pasted onto a piece of wood and then had a few coats of clear coat slapped on thick to give it a paint brush effect.
sentences.zh: 至少有两家供应商出售“手工制作”的绘画，我可以看出，这些绘画是人工智能生成的图片粘贴到一块木头上，然后涂上几层厚厚的透明涂层，使其具有画笔效果。

sentences.en: Also, their banners were AI generated.
sentences.zh: 此外，他们的横幅是人工智能生成的。

sentences.en: Disgustingly dishonest.
sentences.zh: 不诚实得令人厌恶。

blocks.html_en:
It felt much the same when posters went from hand-drawn to created by computers. Personality went from handwriting or eccentric, dynamically-created fonts to Comic Sans. To me this feels like the next level.

TBH, most of those menus just looked like pre-AI menus to me...

sentences.zh: 当海报从手绘变成电脑制作时，感觉很相似。

sentences.zh: 个性从手写字体或古怪的动态创建字体到 Comic Sans。

sentences.en: To me this feels like the next level.TBH, most of those menus just looked like pre-AI menus to me...
sentences.zh: 对我来说，这感觉像是一个新的水平。说实话，大多数菜单对我来说看起来就像是人工智能出现之前的菜单……

blocks.html_en:
I'm aware of that, and it becomes extremely obvious in an inherently soulful context such as a preschool.

Still, while "it's AI generated crap" and variations thereof are all perfectly valid reasons by themselves, they are way, WAY too shallow for the average guy who looks at both versions and prefers the AI one because it looks "better". I would probably sound crazy and/or pretentious trying to explain to him why I prefer the badly drawn one to the perfect AI generated one, I do not know exactly why myself.

sentences.en: I'm aware of that, and it becomes extremely obvious in an inherently soulful context such as a preschool.Still, while "it's AI generated crap" and variations thereof are all perfectly valid reasons by themselves, they are way, WAY too shallow for the average guy who looks at both versions and prefers the AI one because it looks "better".
sentences.zh: 我知道这一点，而且在像幼儿园这样本质上充满灵魂的环境中，这一点变得极其明显。尽管如此，虽然“这是人工智能生成的垃圾”及其变体本身都是完全有效的理由，但对于普通人来说，它们太肤浅了，因为他们看了这两个版本，更喜欢人工智能版本，因为它看起来“更好”。

sentences.en: I would probably sound crazy and/or pretentious trying to explain to him why I prefer the badly drawn one to the perfect AI generated one, I do not know exactly why myself.
sentences.zh: 我可能会听起来很疯狂和/或自命不凡地试图向他解释为什么我更喜欢画得很糟糕的作品而不是完美的人工智能生成的作品，我自己也不知道到底为什么。

sentences.zh: 这帮助了我对那些迂腐的老派 HN 评论的怀旧渴望，现在我似乎很少看到这些评论了……但坦率地说，小时候在游泳池里玩耍的时候经常围绕着假装成某种海洋生物。

blocks.html_en:
The obvious answer is because the old, crudely drawn sign did (and to go deeper, you'd have to ask the janitor who drew it.) Now it's become completely detached from its origin and pointless.

The only reason this question is answerable is because the poster saw the old sign. When everybody is gone who saw the old sign, they'll just continue doing it for no reason. When somebody asks about it, some creep will babble about Chesterton's Fence.

sentences.en: The obvious answer is because the old, crudely drawn sign did (and to go deeper, you'd have to ask the janitor who drew it.)
sentences.zh: 显而易见的答案是因为那个古老的、粗略绘制的标志确实如此（要更深入地了解，您必须询问绘制它的看门人。）

sentences.en: Now it's become completely detached from its origin and pointless.The only reason this question is answerable is because the poster saw the old sign.
sentences.zh: 现在它已经完全脱离了它的起源并且毫无意义。这个问题可以回答的唯一原因是因为发布者看到了旧标志。

sentences.en: When everybody is gone who saw the old sign, they'll just continue doing it for no reason.
sentences.zh: 当所有看到旧标志的人都离开后，他们就会无缘无故地继续这样做。

sentences.en: When somebody asks about it, some creep will babble about Chesterton's Fence.
sentences.zh: 当有人问起这件事时，一些怪人就会喋喋不休地谈论切斯特顿的栅栏。

blocks.html_en: I think AI pictures have this soulless vibe because they are creations of Frankenstein: many pieces of other pictures stitched together in a sloppy manner. A human-made picture, even a bad one, is made by outlining an idea and the presence if this idea behind a picture is what gives it a soul, figuratively speaking.

sentences.en: I think AI pictures have this soulless vibe because they are creations of Frankenstein: many pieces of other pictures stitched together in a sloppy manner.
sentences.zh: 我认为人工智能图片有这种没有灵魂的氛围，因为它们是弗兰肯斯坦的创作：许多其他图片以马虎的方式缝合在一起。

sentences.en: A human-made picture, even a bad one, is made by outlining an idea and the presence if this idea behind a picture is what gives it a soul, figuratively speaking.
sentences.zh: 一幅人造的图片，即使是一张糟糕的图片，也是通过勾勒出一个想法和存在而制成的，如果形象地说，图片背后的这个想法赋予了它灵魂。

blocks.html_en:
It's the corporatization of all aspects of life. It's so bad of a problem that many people voluntarily adopt the style of neutered corporate branding in even their own things. Listen to how people talk: we used to laugh and call people names when they used the word "synergy" in a personal setting and now it's used unironically.

Humans are social creatures. We crave a personal touch in things. That's why we idealize the small shop. But we're also risk averse. It's easier to eat at a big chain restaurant than to take a chance at a local place.

Just look at the country music industry. It's practically an assembly line for "small town nostalgia" slop. And yet small towns are the worst for defecting to Walmart or Dollar General.

sentences.en: It's the corporatization of all aspects of life.
sentences.zh: 这是生活各个方面的公司化。

sentences.en: It's so bad of a problem that many people voluntarily adopt the style of neutered corporate branding in even their own things.
sentences.zh: 这是一个非常糟糕的问题，以至于许多人甚至在自己的事情上自愿采用中性的企业品牌风格。

sentences.en: Listen to how people talk: we used to laugh and call people names when they used the word "synergy" in a personal setting and now it's used unironically.Humans are social creatures.
sentences.zh: 听听人们是如何交谈的：过去，当人们在个人场合使用“协同”这个词时，我们会嘲笑他们并辱骂他们，而现在这个词的使用却带有讽刺意味。人类是社会性动物。

sentences.en: We crave a personal touch in things.
sentences.zh: 我们渴望事物具有个人风格。

sentences.en: That's why we idealize the small shop.
sentences.zh: 这就是为什么我们将小商店理想化。

sentences.en: But we're also risk averse.
sentences.zh: 但我们也规避风险。

sentences.en: It's easier to eat at a big chain restaurant than to take a chance at a local place.Just look at the country music industry.
sentences.zh: 在大型连锁餐厅吃饭比在当地餐馆吃饭更容易。看看乡村音乐产业就知道了。

sentences.en: It's practically an assembly line for "small town nostalgia" slop.
sentences.zh: 它实际上是一条“小镇怀旧”污水的装配线。

sentences.en: And yet small towns are the worst for defecting to Walmart or Dollar General.
sentences.zh: 然而，小城镇最容易叛逃到沃尔玛或达乐百货。

blocks.html_en: Who are these “everyone”? I suspect that you actually mean “many random social media users”, which only proves that getting negative reactions out of the average social media is trivial; not exactly a groundbreaking discovery.

sentences.en: Who are these “everyone”?
sentences.zh: 这些“大家”是谁？

sentences.en: I suspect that you actually mean “many random social media users”, which only proves that getting negative reactions out of the average social media is trivial; not exactly a groundbreaking discovery.
sentences.zh: 我怀疑你实际上指的是“许多随机的社交媒体用户”，这只能证明从普通社交媒体中获得负面反应是微不足道的；不完全是一个突破性的发现。

blocks.html_en:
Sometimes it is, sometimes it isn't.

If it was never about objective quality, Plan 9 from Outer Space and The Eye of Argon would be highly regarded, not the butt of jokes and drinking games.

And those are the examples so infamous that they get to have the honour of being laughed at, there are many more which simply fade into obscurity from their ineptness.

sentences.en: Sometimes it is, sometimes it isn't.If it was never about objective quality, Plan 9 from Outer Space and The Eye of Argon would be highly regarded, not the butt of jokes and drinking games.And those are the examples so infamous that they get to have the honour of being laughed at, there are many more which simply fade into obscurity from their ineptness.
sentences.zh: 有时是，有时不是。如果不考虑客观质量，《外太空9号计划》和《氩之眼》就会受到高度评价，而不是笑话和饮酒游戏的笑柄。这些都是臭名昭著的例子，他们有幸被嘲笑，还有更多的例子只是因为他们的无能而逐渐被遗忘。

blocks.html_en:
i have seen all kinds of menu in my limited time. seeing the example, i would not critique on the choice of tool to "commission" the task for the menu. it would rather comment on the amount of f*cks the restaurant owner gave to getting one done.

seemingly just like the OP i don't hail from japan, where you need to be careful with how you represent the food in your menu. in the DTP days, i have seen random stock images to represent dishes that never makes my table. to me it gives a similar energy.

while everyone has their own technical capabilities, but you still have the final say on what to print. rest is a matter of taste.

sentences.en: i have seen all kinds of menu in my limited time.

blocks.html_en:
I'm fascinated by how AI poster designs have taken over local advertising in seemingly just the last six months - presumably because ChatGPT Images and Gemini Nano Banana finally got good enough at outputting text without obvious defects in the typography.

The posters all look good - much better than their desktop publishing predecessors. And yet they also eat away at the credibility of the event or business for me.

It's hard to trust a poster when the quality of the design has zero relation to the amount of effort that went into creating it.

But is that a widespread feeling, are the vibes bad for regular people, or is this effectively just old man shouting at clouds?

sentences.en: I'm fascinated by how AI poster designs have taken over local advertising in seemingly just the last six months - presumably because ChatGPT Images and Gemini Nano Banana finally got good enough at outputting text without obvious defects in the typography.The posters all look good - much better than their desktop publishing predecessors.
sentences.zh: 我对人工智能海报设计如何在过去六个月内接管本地广告感到着迷 - 大概是因为 ChatGPT Images 和 Gemini Nano Banana 终于在输出文本方面足够好，没有明显的排版缺陷。这些海报看起来都很好 - 比它们的桌面出版前辈好得多。

sentences.en: And yet they also eat away at the credibility of the event or business for me.It's hard to trust a poster when the quality of the design has zero relation to the amount of effort that went into creating it.But is that a widespread feeling, are the vibes bad for regular people, or is this effectively just old man shouting at clouds?
sentences.zh: 然而，它们也削弱了我对活动或生意的可信度。当设计的质量与创作它的努力量为零时，很难相信海报。但这是一种普遍的感觉吗？这种氛围对普通人来说是否有害，或者这实际上只是老人对着云大喊大叫？

blocks.html_en:
I was talking to my daughter a few weeks ago about a coffee shop that was opening soon and I said that based on the posters in the window it probably wouldn’t be very good. She didn’t really understand how I could draw that conclusion and I explained that you could tell that they had been stretched to fit the available dimensions in a way that clearly distorted the images and text. And that like… if you’re careless about one thing like that you’re probably careless about other things.

And I think it’s ultimately the same sort of thing? If in your marketing materials you don’t value craft and you take the shortest path rather than embrace your constraints and produce something good within them, then that’s maybe a negative signal about how you treat the thing you actually do.

IME the people I know who embody great craft in a field… they care about it in everything they do, and they don’t do a thing unless they can do it with care. I think sloppy AI is just a very strong signal of someone who doesn’t follow that.

sentences.en: I was talking to my daughter a few weeks ago about a coffee shop that was opening soon and I said that based on the posters in the window it probably wouldn’t be very good.
sentences.zh: 几周前，我和女儿谈论一家即将开业的咖啡店，我说从橱窗里的海报来看，它可能不会很好。

sentences.en: She didn’t really understand how I could draw that conclusion and I explained that you could tell that they had been stretched to fit the available dimensions in a way that clearly distorted the images and text.
sentences.zh: 她不太明白我如何得出这个结论，我解释说，你可以看出它们已经被拉伸以适应可用的尺寸，从而明显扭曲了图像和文本。

sentences.en: And that like…
sentences.zh: 那就像……

sentences.en: if you’re careless about one thing like that you’re probably careless about other things.And I think it’s ultimately the same sort of thing?
sentences.zh: 如果你对这样的一件事粗心大意，你可能也会对其他事情粗心大意。我认为这最终是同一类事情？

sentences.en: If in your marketing materials you don’t value craft and you take the shortest path rather than embrace your constraints and produce something good within them, then that’s maybe a negative signal about how you treat the thing you actually do.IME the people I know who embody great craft in a field…
sentences.zh: 如果在你的营销材料中，你不重视工艺，而是采取最短的路径，而不是接受你的限制并在其中产生一些好的东西，那么这可能是一个关于你如何对待你实际做的事情的负面信号。我认识的人在某个领域体现了伟大的工艺......

sentences.en: they care about it in everything they do, and they don’t do a thing unless they can do it with care.
sentences.zh: 他们做每件事都很在意，如果他们能用心去做，他们就不会做任何事情。

sentences.en: I think sloppy AI is just a very strong signal of someone who doesn’t follow that.
sentences.zh: 我认为草率的人工智能只是一个非常强烈的信号，表明有人不遵循这一点。

blocks.html_en: This is so definitely not true in many many cases. The best pizza shops in NYC for decades have been quaint, basic and often messy spaces. The ones with the nice menus and overdone decor were often subpar and would fail. This also applies to the best chinese food. Opening a business is hard, you might be incredibly passionate about pizza or coffee but not have budget for a designer. When traveling I usually have way better food at the unpretentious local street food spots rather than the michelin star ones that are all about presentation.

sentences.en: This is so definitely not true in many many cases.
sentences.zh: 在很多情况下这绝对不是真的。

sentences.en: The best pizza shops in NYC for decades have been quaint, basic and often messy spaces.
sentences.zh: 几十年来，纽约市最好的披萨店一直都是古色古香、基本且常常杂乱的空间。

sentences.en: The ones with the nice menus and overdone decor were often subpar and would fail.
sentences.zh: 那些菜单精美、装饰过度的餐厅往往质量不佳，而且会失败。

sentences.en: This also applies to the best chinese food.
sentences.zh: 这也适用于最好的中国菜。

sentences.en: Opening a business is hard, you might be incredibly passionate about pizza or coffee but not have budget for a designer.
sentences.zh: 开办企业很困难，您可能对披萨或咖啡非常热情，但没有设计师的预算。

sentences.en: When traveling I usually have way better food at the unpretentious local street food spots rather than the michelin star ones that are all about presentation.
sentences.zh: 旅行时，我通常会在朴实无华的当地街头小吃店吃到更好的食物，而不是那些注重摆盘的米其林星级餐厅。

blocks.html_en:
> if you’re careless about one thing like that you’re probably careless about other things

I'm not convinced. This rule holds only if the two "things" are related. But one can have a passion for serving the best quality coffee, and be a total wreck in graphic arts.

sentences.en: > if you’re careless about one thing like that you’re probably careless about other thingsI'm not convinced.
sentences.zh: > 如果你对这样的一件事粗心大意，那么你可能对其他事情也粗心大意，我不相信。

sentences.en: This rule holds only if the two "things" are related.
sentences.zh: 仅当两个“事物”相关时，该规则才成立。

sentences.en: But one can have a passion for serving the best quality coffee, and be a total wreck in graphic arts.
sentences.zh: 但一个人可能热衷于提供最优质的咖啡，但却在图形艺术方面彻底失败。

blocks.html_en:
If you're really passionate about serving the best quality coffee, you'll be passionate about the experience around that, and you'll put effort in even if it doesn't pay off.

AI-generated art signals that you didn't put effort in, and so aren't passionate about the experience, and so aren't really passionate about the central thing.

"At least you tried" is not a high standard, but it's the one that AI art fails.

sentences.en: If you're really passionate about serving the best quality coffee, you'll be passionate about the experience around that, and you'll put effort in even if it doesn't pay off.AI-generated art signals that you didn't put effort in, and so aren't passionate about the experience, and so aren't really passionate about the central thing."At least you tried" is not a high standard, but it's the one that AI art fails.
sentences.zh: 如果你真的热衷于提供最优质的咖啡，你就会对围绕它的体验充满热情，即使没有回报，你也会付出努力。人工智能生成的艺术表明你没有付出努力，因此对体验没有热情，因此对中心事物也没有真正的热情。“至少你尝试过”并不是一个高标准，但这是人工智能艺术失败的标准。

blocks.html_en:
While I agree with the general sentiment (people not putting effort is a bad signal), I also think people are sometimes being too harsh. Everybody has limited time and resources and knowledge. Making something (a business, a book, a website, etc.) means making some trade-offs with the resources you got.

When I can easily (without lots of effort/resources) check the quality of something, I prefer not to be that influenced by this type of "bad signals". Think of buying a coffee or trying a sandwich place. When I make a big investment, like a laptop or a TV, yes then it is a different story.

sentences.en: While I agree with the general sentiment (people not putting effort is a bad signal), I also think people are sometimes being too harsh.
sentences.zh: 虽然我同意普遍的观点（人们不付出努力是一个不好的信号），但我也认为人们有时过于严厉。

sentences.en: Everybody has limited time and resources and knowledge.
sentences.zh: 每个人的时间、资源和知识都是有限的。

sentences.en: Making something (a business, a book, a website, etc.) means making some trade-offs with the resources you got.When I can easily (without lots of effort/resources) check the quality of something, I prefer not to be that influenced by this type of "bad signals".
sentences.zh: 做某事（一项业务、一本书、一个网站等）意味着要对你所获得的资源进行一些权衡。当我可以轻松地（无需大量努力/资源）检查某些东西的质量时，我宁愿不受到这种“不良信号”的影响。

sentences.en: Think of buying a coffee or trying a sandwich place.
sentences.zh: 考虑买一杯咖啡或尝试一下三明治。

sentences.en: When I make a big investment, like a laptop or a TV, yes then it is a different story.
sentences.zh: 当我进行大笔投资时，例如笔记本电脑或电视，是的，那么情况就不同了。

blocks.html_en:
I've thought about the specific example of distorted menu photos before as well and to me it also signals intelligence level in the proprietor. I can't imagine myself in that position, looking at that and saying, "yup, that's good." And I also have a hard time imagining not understanding why it looks bad, what one could have done to not make it bad. It feels like a distinct decision to be lazy.

And like you said, if they are that careless and unadept at one thing, how are they going to work the intricacies of an espresso machine and food safety standards? Not that an espresso machine is that hard to operate, but surely it's more difficult than maintaining the aspect ratio of a photo. You don't even need to know the term "aspect ratio" to get it right.

But I also have to admit that my expectations seem to be very off on this specific issue. My wife is quite intelligent (actual, literal genius) and even quite artsy, and yet I had to teach her how to not resize photos incorrectly, both for aspect ratio and quality by resizing too large. It's seems like such an easy thing to see and figure out to me. This must be how my 10 year old sees my chess play.

sentences.en: I've thought about the specific example of distorted menu photos before as well and to me it also signals intelligence level in the proprietor.
sentences.zh: 我之前也考虑过扭曲菜单照片的具体例子，对我来说，这也标志着店主的智力水平。

sentences.en: I can't imagine myself in that position, looking at that and saying, "yup, that's good."
sentences.zh: 我无法想象自己处于那个位置，看着它并说：“是的，那很好。”

sentences.en: And I also have a hard time imagining not understanding why it looks bad, what one could have done to not make it bad.
sentences.zh: 我也很难想象不理解为什么它看起来很糟糕，以及人们可以做些什么来不让它变得糟糕。

sentences.en: It feels like a distinct decision to be lazy.And like you said, if they are that careless and unadept at one thing, how are they going to work the intricacies of an espresso machine and food safety standards?
sentences.zh: 这感觉像是一个明显的懒惰决定。就像你说的，如果他们对一件事如此粗心和不熟练，他们将如何处理复杂的浓缩咖啡机和食品安全标准？

sentences.en: Not that an espresso machine is that hard to operate, but surely it's more difficult than maintaining the aspect ratio of a photo.
sentences.zh: 并不是说浓缩咖啡机很难操作，但它肯定比保持照片的纵横比更困难。

sentences.en: You don't even need to know the term "aspect ratio" to get it right.But I also have to admit that my expectations seem to be very off on this specific issue.
sentences.zh: 您甚至不需要知道“纵横比”这个术语就可以得到正确的结果。但我也必须承认，我对这个具体问题的期望似乎非常偏离。

sentences.en: My wife is quite intelligent (actual, literal genius) and even quite artsy, and yet I had to teach her how to not resize photos incorrectly, both for aspect ratio and quality by resizing too large.
sentences.zh: 我的妻子非常聪明（真正的天才），甚至很有艺术天赋，但我必须教她如何不错误地调整照片大小，无论是宽高比还是质量，因为调整大小太大。

sentences.en: It's seems like such an easy thing to see and figure out to me.
sentences.zh: 对我来说，这似乎是一件很容易看到和弄清楚的事情。

sentences.en: This must be how my 10 year old sees my chess play.
sentences.zh: 我 10 岁的孩子一定是这样看待我下棋的。

blocks.html_en:
I disagree, but it's also academic. Even if every single one of my assumptions is unfair, they're still being made by me and others. Restaurants need to consider the reputational risk caused by customer perception. There's a whole film about it [1].

[1] https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0382932/

sentences.en: I disagree, but it's also academic.
sentences.zh: 我不同意，但这也是学术性的。

sentences.en: Even if every single one of my assumptions is unfair, they're still being made by me and others.
sentences.zh: 即使我的每一项假设都是不公平的，它们仍然是我和其他人做出的。

sentences.en: Restaurants need to consider the reputational risk caused by customer perception.
sentences.zh: 餐厅需要考虑顾客认知带来的声誉风险。

sentences.zh: 一整部电影都是关于它的 [1].[1] https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0382932/

Yea I don’t get the GP/OP sentiment at all.

Some of the best food I ate in places that looked like complete design wrecks. And yes now these places use a lot of really badly made ai art.

The food is still delicious.

blocks.html_en: I think there's a difference between passively and actively putting no thought into something; generating a menu with AI involves deciding that it's worth having but that you don't care about the quality.

sentences.en: I think there's a difference between passively and actively putting no thought into something; generating a menu with AI involves deciding that it's worth having but that you don't care about the quality.
sentences.zh: 我认为被动地和主动地不去思考某事是有区别的。用人工智能生成菜单需要决定它是否值得拥有，但你不关心质量。

blocks.html_en: Generally for restaurants, the better the menu looks, the better the decor, the worse the food is. The 'hole in the wall' places are always the hidden gems. The slick-looking places are always a disappointment.

sentences.en: Generally for restaurants, the better the menu looks, the better the decor, the worse the food is.
sentences.zh: 一般来说，对于餐馆来说，菜单看起来越好，装修越好，但食物越差。

sentences.en: The 'hole in the wall' places are always the hidden gems.
sentences.zh: “墙上的洞”地方总是隐藏的宝石。

sentences.en: The slick-looking places are always a disappointment.
sentences.zh: 那些看似光鲜亮丽的地方，却总是令人失望。

blocks.html_en: I read this quote somewhere: How you do something is how you do everything. I go by this principle in my life. Lately, I have been thinking in how many cases will this be not true.

sentences.en: I read this quote somewhere: How you do something is how you do everything.
sentences.zh: 我在某处读到过这样一句话：你如何做某事，你就如何做每一件事。

sentences.en: I go by this principle in my life.
sentences.zh: 我在生活中遵循这个原则。

sentences.en: Lately, I have been thinking in how many cases will this be not true.
sentences.zh: 最近我一直在想，有多少情况会不是这样。

blocks.html_en:
There is a good chance you will be wrong, but it's not like you will actually check whether or not you were wrong. You are assuming that you are correct, and have already come to the conclusion that the coffee shop is not good. But even if it is good, you will not admit that it is good, because then you will have to admit that you were wrong.

Hopefully, your daughter just politely nodded her head, and did not actually listen to you.

blocks.html_en:
If they're skimping on their posters, what else are they skimping on?

It's cheap, looks cheap, and signals laziness. This is the sentiment I'm getting even from non-technical people on Facebook these days.

sentences.en: If they're skimping on their posters, what else are they skimping on?It's cheap, looks cheap, and signals laziness.
sentences.zh: 如果他们在海报上省钱，那么他们还在什么地方省钱呢？它很便宜，看起来很便宜，而且表明了懒惰。

sentences.en: This is the sentiment I'm getting even from non-technical people on Facebook these days.
sentences.zh: 这就是我最近从 Facebook 上的非技术人员那里得到的看法。

blocks.html_en:
My job did a 'lunch concert'. A violinist and pianist were invited to play during our lunch hour. Great initiative.

And then they notified us by means of an ai-generated poster involving a photorealistic image of a violinist and pianist playing in an office setting. But not the actual artists, no, fake real artists.

And I feel like that is such an insult to the actual artists? Like, these are real, actual people, with real skills, that are doing real work, and apparently that's the same as these two fake non-existant people?

So yeah, I share your sentiment. But I may just be old woman shouting at clouds with the old man.

sentences.en: My job did a 'lunch concert'.
sentences.zh: 我的工作是举办一场“午餐音乐会”。

sentences.en: A violinist and pianist were invited to play during our lunch hour.
sentences.zh: 午餐时间邀请了一位小提琴家和钢琴家来演奏。

sentences.en: Great initiative.And then they notified us by means of an ai-generated poster involving a photorealistic image of a violinist and pianist playing in an office setting.
sentences.zh: 伟大的举措。然后他们通过人工智能生成的海报通知我们，海报上有一张在办公室环境中演奏的小提琴家和钢琴家的逼真图像。

sentences.en: But not the actual artists, no, fake real artists.And I feel like that is such an insult to the actual artists?
sentences.zh: 但不是真正的艺术家，不，假的真正的艺术家。我觉得这对真正的艺术家来说是一种侮辱？

sentences.en: Like, these are real, actual people, with real skills, that are doing real work, and apparently that's the same as these two fake non-existant people?So yeah, I share your sentiment.
sentences.zh: 就像，这些都是真实的、真实的人，拥有真正的技能，正在做真正的工作，显然这和这两个假的不存在的人是一样的？所以是的，我同意你的观点。

sentences.en: But I may just be old woman shouting at clouds with the old man.
sentences.zh: 但我可能只是一个和老人一起对着云喊的老太婆。

blocks.html_en: One of the coolest things about seeing a show at the Fillmore is getting the posters they hand out. A number of bands sell, or have their artists, sell show posters (which then become collectible limited editions); it's way less offensive when it's King Gizzard and the Lizard Wizard than when it's Wizards of the Coast.

sentences.en: One of the coolest things about seeing a show at the Fillmore is getting the posters they hand out.
sentences.zh: 在菲尔莫尔观看演出最酷的事情之一就是获得他们分发的海报。

sentences.en: A number of bands sell, or have their artists, sell show posters (which then become collectible limited editions); it's way less offensive when it's King Gizzard and the Lizard Wizard than when it's Wizards of the Coast.
sentences.zh: 许多乐队出售或让他们的艺术家出售演出海报（然后成为可收藏的限量版）；与海岸奇才相比，砂王王和蜥蜴巫师的攻击性要小得多。

blocks.html_en:
This is a genuine comment, rather than snark, though I appreciate it might initially look that way.

Your comment is an example of something that seems to accrue around AI users: casual, even gleeful, spite. You don't get this with every technology; there is no significant strand of the pro-3D printing community, for example, that goes around taunting people who don't use printers themselves.

It's significant, I think, that this sort of attitude is common around AI, but not other technologies, and it does have a meaningful impact on public attitudes around it. I'm not sure whether it's a result of using AI, or a characteristic that leads to a propensity for AI, but there's something here.

blocks.html_en:
My perspective on this, as someone skeptical of (but also bullish on) AI, is complex and I haven't completely nailed it down.

My knee-jerk reaction is instant credibility loss for the business/person/thing.

Follow-on is sadness (what are we losing in society because of this?), and curiosity: if a polished appearance (even if it is mega plastic) is no longer a sign of quality, then what is? What signals do I need to look for now?

But, wait! Was a high degree of polish ever truly a sign of quality? I've certainly had experiences where a place made every effort to look polished and professional (and generally did a good job at it), but quality missed the mark. In the case of restaurants, a common refrain of mine used to be, "they're trying too hard in the wrong places." Now, I guess, they don't have to try too hard... but they're still not focusing in the right places.

I don't know what to make of it and I go back to still trying to figure out new (or old?) signals for quality.

sentences.en: What signals do I need to look for now?But, wait!
sentences.zh: 我现在需要寻找什么信号？但是，等等！

blocks.html_en:
I have the opposite impression. For me the quality is the cake; polish is just the icing. Putting out a 5-min of effort output with an AI as your image, is all icing and no cake: it has all the right proportions, layout, etc, but something is very wrong with it.

Not sure how diffusion models work in detail so consider this wet neural slop, but I feel like the training data had a sizable portion of it of high quality art, photography, design, and the models captured mostly the polish: the crispiness, the geometry, the contrast: they're after all computable features. It's more difficult to capture the meaningful blurs, the deliberate deviations, and the organic rule breaking. They're properties much harder to "featurize" for, and rely on tags/labeling/descriptions, etc of variable quality.

By all means you can get that with AI anyway, but it also requires you to understand the art itself, and describe it in a way the model produces satisfactory results.

sentences.en: I have the opposite impression.

sentences.en: For me the quality is the cake; polish is just the icing.
sentences.zh: 对我来说，质量就是蛋糕；抛光只是锦上添花。

sentences.en: Putting out a 5-min of effort output with an AI as your image, is all icing and no cake: it has all the right proportions, layout, etc, but something is very wrong with it.Not sure how diffusion models work in detail so consider this wet neural slop, but I feel like the training data had a sizable portion of it of high quality art, photography, design, and the models captured mostly the polish: the crispiness, the geometry, the contrast: they're after all computable features.
sentences.zh: 用人工智能输出 5 分钟的努力输出作为你的图像，只是锦上添花，没有蛋糕：它有所有正确的比例、布局等，但有些东西是非常错误的。不确定扩散模型如何详细工作，所以考虑这个湿神经斜坡，但我觉得训练数据有很大一部分是高质量的艺术、摄影、设计，而模型主要捕捉到了润色：脆度、几何形状、对比度：它们毕竟是可计算的特征。

sentences.en: It's more difficult to capture the meaningful blurs, the deliberate deviations, and the organic rule breaking.
sentences.zh: 捕捉有意义的模糊、故意的偏差和有机的规则破坏更加困难。

sentences.en: They're properties much harder to "featurize" for, and rely on tags/labeling/descriptions, etc of variable quality.By all means you can get that with AI anyway, but it also requires you to understand the art itself, and describe it in a way the model produces satisfactory results.
sentences.zh: 它们的属性很难“特征化”，并且依赖于质量参差不齐的标签/标签/描述等。无论如何，你都可以通过人工智能来实现这一点，但这也需要你了解艺术本身，并以模型产生令人满意的结果的方式描述它。

blocks.html_en:
I have the opposite impression. For me the quality is the cake; polish is just the icing. Putting out a 5-min of effort output with an AI as your image, is all icing and no cake: it has all the right proportions, layout, etc, but something is very wrong with it.

Not sure how diffusion models work in detail so consider this wet neural slop, but I feel like the training data had a sizable portion of it of high quality art, photography, design, and the models captured mostly the polish: the crispiness, the geometry, the contrast: they're after all computable features. It's more difficult to capture the meaningful blurs, the deliberate deviations, and the organic rule breaking. They're properties much harder to "featurize" for, and rely on tags/labeling/descriptions, etc of variable quality.

By all means you can get that with AI anyway, but it also requires you to understand the art itself, and describe it in a way the model produces satisfactory results.

blocks.html_en:
> if a polished appearance (even if it is mega plastic) is no longer a sign of quality, then what is? What signals do I need to look for now?

AI produces apparent uniformity, it makes it harder to distinguish the quality of the underlying process. Same thing happens in all fields, we are all harder to differentiate, then why would you pick me over the others? this is the hard question we are facing now, and it's a desperate situation to be in. Not just artists and people looking for a job, but also companies to their investors and products to customers. All harder to tell apart.

I think this drives a lot of the AI craze. If I can't own my niche anymore, what is my new specialization? We have no answers yet. We have seen this kind of total competition in a domain with cheap imitation in SEO and slop writing.

sentences.en: > if a polished appearance (even if it is mega plastic) is no longer a sign of quality, then what is?
sentences.zh: > 如果抛光的外观（即使是巨型塑料）不再是质量的标志，那么什么才是？

sentences.en: What signals do I need to look for now?AI produces apparent uniformity, it makes it harder to distinguish the quality of the underlying process.
sentences.zh: 我现在需要寻找哪些信号？人工智能产生明显的一致性，这使得区分底层流程的质量变得更加困难。

sentences.en: Same thing happens in all fields, we are all harder to differentiate, then why would you pick me over the others?
sentences.zh: 同样的事情发生在所有领域，我们都很难区分，那为什么你会选择我而不是其他人呢？

sentences.en: this is the hard question we are facing now, and it's a desperate situation to be in.
sentences.zh: 这是我们现在面临的难题，也是一个令人绝望的处境。

sentences.zh: 一切都很难区分。我认为这推动了很多人工智能热潮。

sentences.en: If I can't own my niche anymore, what is my new specialization?
sentences.zh: 如果我不能再拥有自己的利基市场，我的新专业是什么？

sentences.en: We have no answers yet.

blocks.html_en:
Sure, but it means that they managed to pick third-parties they work with that give a shit. They could have always picked ones that didn't.

When you're buying a pig in a poke (which is always the case when you're a first-time customer), this is a positive signal.

blocks.html_en:
> have the ability to look at a poster and instantly clock that it was AI-generated?

But soulless corporate bullshit was the same before "AI". It doesn't matter how it was produced, it's just that "AI" does it faster and cheaper.

So it doesn't have to be machine generated, just looking like it is is enough.

sentences.zh: > 有能力看到一张海报并立即判断它是人工智能生成的吗？但毫无灵魂的企业废话在“人工智能”之前是一样的。

sentences.en: It doesn't matter how it was produced, it's just that "AI" does it faster and cheaper.So it doesn't have to be machine generated, just looking like it is is enough.
sentences.zh: 它是如何产生的并不重要，重要的是“AI”做得更快、更便宜。所以它不必是机器生成的，只要看起来就足够了。

blocks.html_en:
> have the ability to look at a poster and instantly clock that it was AI-generated?

But soulless corporate bullshit was the same before "AI". It doesn't matter how it was produced, it's just that "AI" does it faster and cheaper.

So it doesn't have to be machine generated, just looking like it is is enough.

blocks.html_en:
I agree with others saying that this doesn't disqualify complaints.

In fact, it can be precisely because they can't tell the difference that they would be concerned about consuming AI outputs when they don't know they are. If someone wants to take a stand and only read human text and human art then we may need regulation to add notices when text and images are LLM outputs.

sentences.en: I agree with others saying that this doesn't disqualify complaints.In fact, it can be precisely because they can't tell the difference that they would be concerned about consuming AI outputs when they don't know they are.
sentences.zh: 我同意其他人的说法，这并不能取消投诉资格。事实上，正是因为他们无法区分其中的差异，他们才会担心在不知道的情况下消耗人工智能输出。

sentences.en: If someone wants to take a stand and only read human text and human art then we may need regulation to add notices when text and images are LLM outputs.
sentences.zh: 如果有人想表明立场，只阅读人类文本和人类艺术，那么我们可能需要制定法规，在文本和图像是法学硕士输出时添加通知。

blocks.html_en: What's the argument here? People don't like spit in their food, how many people can tell their food got spat in? Does that mean they're somehow wrong or to be belittled for not liking spit in their food?

sentences.en: What's the argument here?
sentences.zh: 这里的论点是什么？

sentences.en: People don't like spit in their food, how many people can tell their food got spat in?
sentences.zh: 人们不喜欢在食物中吐口水，有多少人能看出自己的食物被吐了呢？

sentences.en: Does that mean they're somehow wrong or to be belittled for not liking spit in their food?
sentences.zh: 这是否意味着他们在某种程度上是错误的，或者因为不喜欢在食物中吐口水而受到贬低？

blocks.html_en: It's not an argument, it's an open question: do people who express a dislike of AI know when they see a poster that was generated with assistance from AI?

sentences.en: It's not an argument, it's an open question: do people who express a dislike of AI know when they see a poster that was generated with assistance from AI?
sentences.zh: 这不是一个争论，而是一个悬而未决的问题：那些表达不喜欢人工智能的人在看到人工智能帮助生成的海报时是否知道？

blocks.html_en:
I meant to ask what the point of the question is basically. What would change, what would it matter for, if the answer was yes or no?

Besides it being so general a question to really be useless as anything but an insinuation IMO, which is why I'm trying to tease that insinuation out. E.g. are people who use Apple left-handed? Are they gay? The answer is "yes, some of them, of course, but also many aren't... why are you asking?"

There is no thing as "just a question, nothing in mind really". That does not exist. The mind that can ask a question cannot arrive at it in a vacuum.

sentences.en: I meant to ask what the point of the question is basically.
sentences.zh: 我本来想问这个问题的重点是什么。

sentences.en: What would change, what would it matter for, if the answer was yes or no?Besides it being so general a question to really be useless as anything but an insinuation IMO, which is why I'm trying to tease that insinuation out.
sentences.zh: 如果答案是是或否，会发生什么变化，这有什么关系？除了在我看来，这是一个如此普遍的问题，除了影射之外没有任何用处，这就是为什么我试图梳理出这种影射。

sentences.en: E.g.
sentences.zh: 例如。

sentences.en: are people who use Apple left-handed?
sentences.zh: 使用苹果电脑的人都是左撇子吗？

sentences.en: Are they gay?
sentences.zh: 他们是同性恋吗？

sentences.en: The answer is "yes, some of them, of course, but also many aren't...
sentences.zh: 答案是“是的，当然，其中一些，但也有很多不是......

sentences.en: why are you asking?"There is no thing as "just a question, nothing in mind really".
sentences.zh: 你为什么问这个？”没有什么事情是“只是一个问题，实际上什么也没考虑”。

sentences.en: That does not exist.
sentences.zh: 那是不存在的。

sentences.en: The mind that can ask a question cannot arrive at it in a vacuum.
sentences.zh: 能够提出问题的头脑无法在真空中得到它。

blocks.html_en: OK, my point here is that a YouGov poll about people's attitude to AI may not tell us anything actionable about how people feel about AI posters - it depends on how good they are at detecting them.

sentences.en: OK, my point here is that a YouGov poll about people's attitude to AI may not tell us anything actionable about how people feel about AI posters - it depends on how good they are at detecting them.
sentences.zh: 好吧，我的观点是，一项关于人们对人工智能态度的 YouGov 民意调查可能不会告诉我们任何关于人们对人工智能海报的看法的可操作信息——这取决于他们检测海报的能力。

blocks.html_en:
Almost none, sadly. Most of the population just doesn't have that skill, which is why places like Facebook are just reams of AI Jesus upvoted to the Moon.

I saw this menu online the other day, and it's not even discernable from the photo what the food products are; it even sets off trypophobia:

https://imgur.com/a/YvyRjts

But people are still ordering it.

sentences.zh: 可惜几乎没有。

blocks.html_en: I agree. For most people I've talked to, when this topic comes up, they'll tell me they don't like AI, but then cannot really identify it. Especially the elderly folks in my neighborhood. You look at their facebook feeds and it's 100% slop. No joke. Every. Single. Post. Is. AI. Slop. And for the most part they can't tell.

blocks.html_en:
> how AI poster designs have taken over local advertising

Tale as old as time: a race to the bottom. Businesses trying to cut corners, thinking they can offset it against losses from exiting customers. If it wasn't, a lot will just cut more corners.

blocks.html_en:
I don't think it's a corner cutting thing though.

These businesses weren't paying someone to make a flier. The business owner was sweating for an hour in Microsoft Word and producing something that looked terrible.

I expect they are now investing approximately the same about of time iterating in ChatGPT and producing something that genuinely looks like a huge improvement to them.

sentences.en: I don't think it's a corner cutting thing though.These businesses weren't paying someone to make a flier.
sentences.zh: 但我不认为这是一个偷工减料的事情。这些企业并没有花钱请人制作传单。

sentences.en: The business owner was sweating for an hour in Microsoft Word and producing something that looked terrible.I expect they are now investing approximately the same about of time iterating in ChatGPT and producing something that genuinely looks like a huge improvement to them.
sentences.zh: 企业主在 Microsoft Word 中汗流浃背地工作了一个小时，并制作出了一些看起来很糟糕的东西。我预计他们现在会投入大约相同的时间在 ChatGPT 上进行迭代，并制作出对他们来说真正看起来像是巨大改进的东西。

blocks.html_en:
This.

I said it elsewhere in this thread differently but people want to pretend the options are:

1. Paying a local designer to make a bespoke beautiful poster

OR

2. Use AI

It's not, the money is often just not there to pay someone. So you get whatever the intern or the owner can slap together in whatever tool they know best (which is rarely the best tool for the job).

It's completely understandable that they would reach for LLMs to help improve their posters.

So many people are anti-AI/anti-LLM just because it is AI/LLM, not because it's objectively worse than the alternative. It's tiring.

It's the Electron vs Native argument all over again. Sure, we'd all love hand-crafted, beautiful, low-resource native applications for everything, that takes a lot of time and/or money. It's a decision between Native and Electron, it's a decision between Electron or nothing (or at best only 1 OS supported).

sentences.en: This.I said it elsewhere in this thread differently but people want to pretend the options are:1.
sentences.zh: 我在这个帖子的其他地方说了不同的话，但人们想假装选项是：1。

sentences.en: Paying a local designer to make a bespoke beautiful posterOR2.
sentences.zh: 付钱请当地设计师制作一张定制的漂亮海报OR2。

sentences.en: Use AIIt's not, the money is often just not there to pay someone.
sentences.zh: 用 AI 不行，钱往往就是没有钱付给别人。

sentences.en: So you get whatever the intern or the owner can slap together in whatever tool they know best (which is rarely the best tool for the job).It's completely understandable that they would reach for LLMs to help improve their posters.So many people are anti-AI/anti-LLM just because it is AI/LLM, not because it's objectively worse than the alternative.
sentences.zh: 因此，你可以得到实习生或老板可以用他们最了解的任何工具组合在一起的任何东西（这很少是最适合这项工作的工具）。他们会寻求法学硕士来帮助改进他们的海报是完全可以理解的。很多人反对人工智能/反法学硕士只是因为它是人工智能/法学硕士，而不是因为它客观上比其他选择更糟糕。

sentences.en: It's tiring.It's the Electron vs Native argument all over again.
sentences.zh: 这很累。这又是 Electron 与 Native 的争论了。

sentences.en: Sure, we'd all love hand-crafted, beautiful, low-resource native applications for everything, that takes a lot of time and/or money.
sentences.zh: 当然，我们都喜欢手工制作的、漂亮的、低资源的本机应用程序，这需要花费大量的时间和/或金钱。

sentences.en: It's a decision between Native and Electron, it's a decision between Electron or nothing (or at best only 1 OS supported).
sentences.zh: 这是 Native 和 Electron 之间的决定，是 Electron 或什么都不做（或者最多只支持 1 个操作系统）之间的决定。

> I said it elsewhere in this thread differently but people want to pretend the options are:

> 1. Paying a local designer to make a bespoke beautiful poster

> OR

> 2. Use AI

You are wrong and trying to make people you disagree with sound stupid. Stop it.

I’ll tell you then that the options I believe in are:

1. Paying a local designer to make a bespoke beautiful poster

2. don’t

3. Use AI

And I accept both option 1 and 2.

blocks.html_en:
Lost me at the last paragraph. Comparing billion dollar companies to small businesses does not make sense.

The real reason for Electron apps being a thing is that there is very little competition where they are used. If chatting applications could properly communicate with each other based on international standards instead of being silos, nobody in their right mind would use the trash clients that Slack and Whatsapp produce, for instance.

sentences.en: Lost me at the last paragraph.
sentences.zh: 最后一段让我迷失了。

sentences.en: Comparing billion dollar companies to small businesses does not make sense.The real reason for Electron apps being a thing is that there is very little competition where they are used.
sentences.zh: 将价值数十亿美元的公司与小企业进行比较是没有意义的。Electron 应用程序之所以受欢迎的真正原因是，使用它们的地方几乎没有竞争。

sentences.en: If chatting applications could properly communicate with each other based on international standards instead of being silos, nobody in their right mind would use the trash clients that Slack and Whatsapp produce, for instance.
sentences.zh: 例如，如果聊天应用程序能够根据国际标准正确地相互通信而不是孤岛，那么任何头脑正常的人都不会使用 Slack 和 Whatsapp 生产的垃圾客户端。

blocks.html_en:
Lost me at the last paragraph. Comparing billion dollar companies to small businesses does not make sense.

The real reason for Electron apps being a thing is that there is very little competition where they are used. If chatting applications could properly communicate with each other based on international standards instead of being silos, nobody in their right mind would use the trash clients that Slack and Whatsapp produce, for instance.

blocks.html_en:
> So many people are anti-AI/anti-LLM just because it is AI/LLM, not because it's objectively worse than the alternative. It's tiring.

no, it's because the SNR of AI slop is worse than human creations.

  hamburger: $4.99
  S̵u̸p̸e̷r̷s̶l̵o̷p̵ ̸S̶i̴g̸n̷a̴t̸u̷r̵e̵b̷u̷r̶g̵e̸r̶:̶ ̷$̴4̸.̴9̸9̷(̵b̴a̵s̷e̸ ̵c̴o̸n̵f̷i̶g̶u̵r̶a̵t̴i̴o̸n̴)̴

  The amount of data hasn't changed - it's presentation. The noise is real. Presenting just the prompt to patrons may suffice, but dressing it up with an LLM output is mast^C

I mean, to say something at least somewhat constructive sounding, they could put an EEG cap on a person and show them bunch of AI slop so to establish a vector and a bias term for less offensive and more focused feeling AI generated data, but I don't believe they're doing it.

sentences.en: > So many people are anti-AI/anti-LLM just because it is AI/LLM, not because it's objectively worse than the alternative.
sentences.zh: > 很多人反对人工智能/反法学硕士只是因为它是人工智能/法学硕士，而不是因为它客观上比替代方案更糟糕。

sentences.en: It's tiring.no, it's because the SNR of AI slop is worse than human creations.
sentences.zh: 很累。不，是因为AI slop的SNR比人类创造的还差。

sentences.en: Presenting just the prompt to patrons may suffice, but dressing it up with an LLM output is mast^C I mean, to say something at least somewhat constructive sounding, they could put an EEG cap on a person and show them bunch of AI slop so to establish a vector and a bias term for less offensive and more focused feeling AI generated data, but I don't believe they're doing it.

blocks.html_en:
What's weird here is that I should be in total agreement with you.

I love it when AI tools are used to give people the ability to solve problems that previously they could not solve well.

For the fliers I'm talking about here (think a hardware store promoting their summer sale) I don't think the impact on the local poster design community is meaningful.

And yet... the resulting posters put me off. I'm trying to figure out why that is. I think the best explanation I have is the lack of intention behind them - knowing that none of the visual details - the illustrations, the typographic flair - had anyone thinking about them while they were constructed.

sentences.en: What's weird here is that I should be in total agreement with you.I love it when AI tools are used to give people the ability to solve problems that previously they could not solve well.For the fliers I'm talking about here (think a hardware store promoting their summer sale) I don't think the impact on the local poster design community is meaningful.And yet...
sentences.zh: 奇怪的是，我应该完全同意你的观点。我喜欢人工智能工具让人们能够解决以前无法解决的问题。对于我在这里谈论的传单（想想宣传夏季促销的五金店），我认为这对当地海报设计社区的影响没有意义。然而......

sentences.en: the resulting posters put me off.
sentences.zh: 由此产生的海报让我感到厌烦。

sentences.en: I'm trying to figure out why that is.
sentences.zh: 我正在尝试找出原因。

sentences.en: I think the best explanation I have is the lack of intention behind them - knowing that none of the visual details - the illustrations, the typographic flair - had anyone thinking about them while they were constructed.
sentences.zh: 我认为最好的解释是它们背后缺乏意图 - 知道没有任何视觉细节 - 插图、印刷风格 - 在它们被构造时没有人考虑它们。

blocks.html_en:
> And yet... the resulting posters put me off. I'm trying to figure out why that is. I think the best explanation I have is the lack of intention behind them - knowing that none of the visual details - the illustrations, the typographic flair - had anyone thinking about them while they were constructed.

I think that's exactly it, and it's not really hard to articulate. You are simply noticing the lack of effort, lack of care, and lack of craftsmanship that is inherent in AI-produced slop. This isn't mysticism and it doesn't take a sixth sense to detect. When someone puts no effort into creating something, that lack of effort carries through the entire work, and is (or should be) obvious and easy to spot.

Ask a schoolteacher. They can spot when someone half-asses a term paper, and, at least I hope, they can spot AI slop term papers.

blocks.html_en:
> And yet... the resulting posters put me off. I'm trying to figure out why that is. I think the best explanation I have is the lack of intention behind them - knowing that none of the visual details - the illustrations, the typographic flair - had anyone thinking about them while they were constructed.

I think that's exactly it, and it's not really hard to articulate. You are simply noticing the lack of effort, lack of care, and lack of craftsmanship that is inherent in AI-produced slop. This isn't mysticism and it doesn't take a sixth sense to detect. When someone puts no effort into creating something, that lack of effort carries through the entire work, and is (or should be) obvious and easy to spot.

Ask a schoolteacher. They can spot when someone half-asses a term paper, and, at least I hope, they can spot AI slop term papers.

blocks.html_en:
Okay I've been thinking about this a lot lately, and here's my "I'm currently drinking my coffee" take on this. I'm willing to be significantly wrong here, but for the sake of discussion, here's where I think I'm at.

I also agree with

> I love it when AI tools are used to give people the ability to solve problems that previously they could not solve well.

But I also really hate these menus, I see them all the time as well. I think that my conclusion is slightly different than yours: in my opinion, just not a lot of people have taste. That's sort of like, true by the definition of taste, but what I mean is something more specific: it's not because of AI that I dislike these signs. It's because of what you said above:

> producing something that genuinely looks like a huge improvement to them.

While it looks like a huge improvement to them, it does not to me. Our tastes differ.

Here's a version of this that doesn't involve AI at all: last night I ate sushi at a place I'd never been before. Their menu was a bit chaotic, and had a bunch of annotations, along with a guide to those annotations. One section had both "2" and "2 pieces" listed under the options, and so I was confused: what was the difference here? Turns out it's basically just a typo, both mean that. Combine that with some spelling mistakes, and yeah, I had a pretty bad impression of the menu. The sushi was fine though, I'd go back. But this wasn't a super low-end place, so the attention to detail being off made me a bit worried before I actually ate the food.

Same problem, same issue, AI is really the only variable here. And it is the natural downside of "democratization" or "helping more people do more things", naturally, there will be a lot more low-quality stuff out there than before. That's not actually inherently a bad thing. But it can feel weird.

blocks.html_en:
I guess I see very little "soul" in most marketing/advertising in the first place. It was slop well before LLMs entered the scene. If my local hardware store wants to put up a poster for their summer sale I don't care if it's black comic sans on white paper or if it's a AI-generated advertisement. Either way I'm not looking that closely at it and neither version were going to show up in an art museum or recieve design accolades.

There is so little intention in anything (again, even before LLMs) so I don't begrudge places for using LLMs to try and produce similar quality to what larger chains/corps are able to commision.

I see this as similar to things like people wanting "clean ingredients" or "sustainable packaging" or "healthy food" in restaurants/bakeries/etc. They _say_ they want that but they are not at all willing to _pay_ for it. To be clear, I do wish those things were the baseline, but they are not and unfortunately it is near-impossible to compete with other businesses that don't care about those things. At the end of the day consumers see "$15 hamburger" vs "$10 hamburger", the better/fewer ingredients, the better-paid labor, the recyclable packaging, etc simple don't matter. This is _not_ an argument to underpay workers, kill the environment, and fill your hamburgers with sawdust, it's simply a statement on how hard it is to compete when consumers very rarely reward companies for doing things "right" no matter how much lip-service they pay to it.

I've watched a close relative have people say "you should do X thing!" where X is something like "fewer ingredients" or "low calorie" or "healthy"-type things, only to see sales crater after an initial boost. People try it, they feel good about themselves, then they go right back to buying the thing packed with sugar.

There is a wide gulf between "what people say that want" and "how they actually behave". LLMs are currently in a category where people can have visceral reaction and claim "I'd never buy something from a company that does that" but I can tell you right now that won't last. Be anti-bad-design, be anti-bad-code but don't be anti-LLM because that opinion isn't going to age well (and yes, I know _you_ simonw, aren't anti-LLM, I'm speaking generally).

sentences.en: I guess I see very little "soul" in most marketing/advertising in the first place.
sentences.zh: 我想我一开始在大多数营销/广告中看到的“灵魂”很少。

sentences.en: It was slop well before LLMs entered the scene.
sentences.zh: 早在法学硕士进入现场之前，它就已经很糟糕了。

sentences.en: If my local hardware store wants to put up a poster for their summer sale I don't care if it's black comic sans on white paper or if it's a AI-generated advertisement.
sentences.zh: 如果我当地的五金店想为夏季促销贴一张海报，我不在乎它是白纸上的黑色漫画还是人工智能生成的广告。

sentences.en: Either way I'm not looking that closely at it and neither version were going to show up in an art museum or recieve design accolades.There is so little intention in anything (again, even before LLMs) so I don't begrudge places for using LLMs to try and produce similar quality to what larger chains/corps are able to commision.I see this as similar to things like people wanting "clean ingredients" or "sustainable packaging" or "healthy food" in restaurants/bakeries/etc. They _say_ they want that but they are not at all willing to _pay_ for it.
sentences.zh: 无论哪种方式，我都没有仔细观察它，并且两个版本都不会出现在艺术博物馆或获得设计荣誉。任何事情都没有什么意图（同样，即使在法学硕士之前），所以我不嫉妒使用法学硕士尝试生产与大型连锁店/企业能够委托的类似质量的地方。我认为这类似于人们想要“清洁成分”或“可持续包装”或“健康食品”之类的事情餐馆/面包店/等他们说他们想要这样，但他们根本不愿意为此付出代价。

sentences.en: To be clear, I do wish those things were the baseline, but they are not and unfortunately it is near-impossible to compete with other businesses that don't care about those things.
sentences.zh: 需要明确的是，我确实希望这些事情是基线，但事实并非如此，不幸的是，几乎不可能与其他不关心这些事情的企业竞争。

sentences.en: At the end of the day consumers see "$15 hamburger" vs "$10 hamburger", the better/fewer ingredients, the better-paid labor, the recyclable packaging, etc simple don't matter.
sentences.zh: 归根结底，消费者会看到“15 美元汉堡”与“10 美元汉堡”，成分更好/更少、工资更高、包装可回收等简单的因素并不重要。

sentences.en: This is _not_ an argument to underpay workers, kill the environment, and fill your hamburgers with sawdust, it's simply a statement on how hard it is to compete when consumers very rarely reward companies for doing things "right" no matter how much lip-service they pay to it.I've watched a close relative have people say "you should do X thing!"
sentences.zh: 这并不是反对低薪工人、破坏环境、在汉堡包里塞满锯末的论点，它只是表明，当消费者很少奖励那些做“正确”事情的公司，无论他们在口头上付出多少代价时，竞争是多么困难。我见过一个近亲让人们说“你应该做某件事！”

sentences.en: where X is something like "fewer ingredients" or "low calorie" or "healthy"-type things, only to see sales crater after an initial boost.
sentences.zh: 其中 X 是“更少成分”或“低热量”或“健康”之类的东西，但在最初的推动后销量却急剧下降。

sentences.en: People try it, they feel good about themselves, then they go right back to buying the thing packed with sugar.There is a wide gulf between "what people say that want" and "how they actually behave".
sentences.zh: 人们尝试一下，自我感觉良好，然后就又回去购买含糖的东西。“人们所说的想要的东西”和“他们实际的行为方式”之间存在着巨大的鸿沟。

sentences.en: LLMs are currently in a category where people can have visceral reaction and claim "I'd never buy something from a company that does that" but I can tell you right now that won't last.
sentences.zh: 目前，法学硕士属于人们可能会产生本能反应并声称“我永远不会从这样做的公司购买东西”的类别，但我现在可以告诉你，这种情况不会持续太久。

sentences.en: Be anti-bad-design, be anti-bad-code but don't be anti-LLM because that opinion isn't going to age well (and yes, I know _you_ simonw, aren't anti-LLM, I'm speaking generally).
sentences.zh: 反对糟糕的设计，反对糟糕的代码，但不要反对LLM，因为这种观点不会很好地流行（是的，我知道_you_simonw，不是反对LLM，我一般来说）。

blocks.html_en:
I guess I see very little "soul" in most marketing/advertising in the first place. It was slop well before LLMs entered the scene. If my local hardware store wants to put up a poster for their summer sale I don't care if it's black comic sans on white paper or if it's a AI-generated advertisement. Either way I'm not looking that closely at it and neither version were going to show up in an art museum or recieve design accolades.

There is so little intention in anything (again, even before LLMs) so I don't begrudge places for using LLMs to try and produce similar quality to what larger chains/corps are able to commision.

I see this as similar to things like people wanting "clean ingredients" or "sustainable packaging" or "healthy food" in restaurants/bakeries/etc. They _say_ they want that but they are not at all willing to _pay_ for it. To be clear, I do wish those things were the baseline, but they are not and unfortunately it is near-impossible to compete with other businesses that don't care about those things. At the end of the day consumers see "$15 hamburger" vs "$10 hamburger", the better/fewer ingredients, the better-paid labor, the recyclable packaging, etc simple don't matter. This is _not_ an argument to underpay workers, kill the environment, and fill your hamburgers with sawdust, it's simply a statement on how hard it is to compete when consumers very rarely reward companies for doing things "right" no matter how much lip-service they pay to it.

I've watched a close relative have people say "you should do X thing!" where X is something like "fewer ingredients" or "low calorie" or "healthy"-type things, only to see sales crater after an initial boost. People try it, they feel good about themselves, then they go right back to buying the thing packed with sugar.

There is a wide gulf between "what people say that want" and "how they actually behave". LLMs are currently in a category where people can have visceral reaction and claim "I'd never buy something from a company that does that" but I can tell you right now that won't last. Be anti-bad-design, be anti-bad-code but don't be anti-LLM because that opinion isn't going to age well (and yes, I know _you_ simonw, aren't anti-LLM, I'm speaking generally).

blocks.html_en:
> LLMs are currently in a category where people can have visceral reaction and claim "I'd never buy something from a company that does that" but I can tell you right now that won't last.

Because people are struggling to make ends meet as wealth gets more and more concentrated. It reminds me of that really disgusting joke: why do sheep farmers like to fuck their sheep just at the edge of a cliff? Because they push back.

So yeah, people often make choices they'd rather not make. Doesn't mean anything, just like that sheep doesn't love the farmer and is grinding up against him, it doesn't want to fall of a cliff and die.

And these companies take a few cherry picked snapshots, never show the video, never show the aftermath, and brag about acceptance. Fuck all of that, so much.

People don't want all of humanity to be stolen from by companies who then turn around and sue you if you violate their copyright. That settles all of it, already. All this stuff about quality and economics is irrelevant. We hate people who steal from the little man to enrich the wealthy, always did, always will. Where there is a "we" that isn't just isolated people playing pretend, you will find this as the bedrock, maybe a layer above hating child abuse. And making the "accept child abuse or robber barons" option 10%, 20%, 50000% cheaper doesn't change that in the slightest.

I'll go you one further: the very few ones who do, or don't care, don't matter, because when it comes to ethics and society they already checked out, and can stay checked out. They got old, they lost what they were supposed to hold on to if they ever had it, and that's tragic, but mostly their problem which they want to extend onto the people who don't have it.

sentences.en: > LLMs are currently in a category where people can have visceral reaction and claim "I'd never buy something from a company that does that" but I can tell you right now that won't last.Because people are struggling to make ends meet as wealth gets more and more concentrated.
sentences.zh: > 目前，法学硕士属于这样一个类别，人们可能会有本能的反应，并声称“我永远不会从这样做的公司购买东西”，但我现在可以告诉你，这种情况不会持续太久。因为随着财富越来越集中，人们正在努力维持收支平衡。

sentences.en: It reminds me of that really disgusting joke: why do sheep farmers like to fuck their sheep just at the edge of a cliff?
sentences.zh: 这让我想起了那个非常恶心的笑话：为什么养羊的农民喜欢在悬崖边操他们的羊？

sentences.en: Because they push back.So yeah, people often make choices they'd rather not make.
sentences.zh: 因为他们会反击。所以，是的，人们经常做出他们宁愿不做的选择。

sentences.en: Doesn't mean anything, just like that sheep doesn't love the farmer and is grinding up against him, it doesn't want to fall of a cliff and die.And these companies take a few cherry picked snapshots, never show the video, never show the aftermath, and brag about acceptance.
sentences.zh: 没有任何意义，就像那只羊不爱农夫并与他对抗一样，它不想从悬崖上掉下来而死。而这些公司只拍几张精选的快照，从不展示视频，从不展示后果，并吹嘘接受。

sentences.en: Fuck all of that, so much.People don't want all of humanity to be stolen from by companies who then turn around and sue you if you violate their copyright.
sentences.zh: 去他妈的这一切，太多了。人们不希望所有的人性都被公司窃取，如果你侵犯了他们的版权，这些公司就会转而起诉你。

sentences.en: That settles all of it, already.
sentences.zh: 这一切都已经解决了。

sentences.en: All this stuff about quality and economics is irrelevant.
sentences.zh: 所有这些关于质量和经济的东西都是无关紧要的。

sentences.en: We hate people who steal from the little man to enrich the wealthy, always did, always will.
sentences.zh: 我们讨厌那些从小人物那里偷窃来让富人致富的人，过去、现在、将来都会如此。

sentences.en: Where there is a "we" that isn't just isolated people playing pretend, you will find this as the bedrock, maybe a layer above hating child abuse.
sentences.zh: 如果有一个“我们”，而不仅仅是孤立的人在玩假装，你会发现这是基石，也许比讨厌虐待儿童更重要。

sentences.en: And making the "accept child abuse or robber barons" option 10%, 20%, 50000% cheaper doesn't change that in the slightest.I'll go you one further: the very few ones who do, or don't care, don't matter, because when it comes to ethics and society they already checked out, and can stay checked out.
sentences.zh: 让“接受虐待儿童或强盗大亨”选项便宜10%、20%、50000%并不会改变这一点。我会进一步告诉你：极少数人关心或不关心，并不重要，因为当涉及到道德和社会时，他们已经检查了，并且可以保持检查。

sentences.en: They got old, they lost what they were supposed to hold on to if they ever had it, and that's tragic, but mostly their problem which they want to extend onto the people who don't have it.
sentences.zh: 他们变老了，他们失去了原本拥有的东西，这是悲剧，但主要是他们的问题，他们想把这个问题延伸到那些没有它的人身上。

sentences.zh: > 目前，法学硕士属于这样一个类别，人们可能会有本能的反应，并声称“我永远不会从这样做的公司购买东西”，但我现在可以告诉你，这种情况不会持续太久。因为随着财富越来越集中，人们正在努力维持收支平衡。

sentences.en: It reminds me of that really disgusting joke: why do sheep farmers like to fuck their sheep just at the edge of a cliff?
sentences.zh: 这让我想起了那个非常恶心的笑话：为什么养羊的农民喜欢在悬崖边操他们的羊？

sentences.en: Because they push back.So yeah, people often make choices they'd rather not make.
sentences.zh: 因为他们会反击。所以，是的，人们经常做出他们宁愿不做的选择。

sentences.en: Doesn't mean anything, just like that sheep doesn't love the farmer and is grinding up against him, it doesn't want to fall of a cliff and die.And these companies take a few cherry picked snapshots, never show the video, never show the aftermath, and brag about acceptance.
sentences.zh: 没有任何意义，就像那只羊不爱农夫并与他对抗一样，它不想从悬崖上掉下来而死。而这些公司只拍几张精选的快照，从不展示视频，从不展示后果，并吹嘘接受。

sentences.en: Fuck all of that, so much.People don't want all of humanity to be stolen from by companies who then turn around and sue you if you violate their copyright.
sentences.zh: 去他妈的这一切，太多了。人们不希望所有的人性都被公司窃取，如果你侵犯了他们的版权，这些公司就会转而起诉你。

sentences.en: That settles all of it, already.
sentences.zh: 这一切都已经解决了。

sentences.en: All this stuff about quality and economics is irrelevant.
sentences.zh: 所有这些关于质量和经济的东西都是无关紧要的。

sentences.en: We hate people who steal from the little man to enrich the wealthy, always did, always will.
sentences.zh: 我们讨厌那些从小人物那里偷窃来让富人致富的人，过去、现在、将来都会如此。

sentences.en: Where there is a "we" that isn't just isolated people playing pretend, you will find this as the bedrock, maybe a layer above hating child abuse.
sentences.zh: 如果有一个“我们”，而不仅仅是孤立的人在玩假装，你会发现这是基石，也许比讨厌虐待儿童更重要。

sentences.en: And making the "accept child abuse or robber barons" option 10%, 20%, 50000% cheaper doesn't change that in the slightest.I'll go you one further: the very few ones who do, or don't care, don't matter, because when it comes to ethics and society they already checked out, and can stay checked out.
sentences.zh: 让“接受虐待儿童或强盗大亨”选项便宜10%、20%、50000%并不会改变这一点。我会进一步告诉你：极少数人关心或不关心，并不重要，因为当涉及到道德和社会时，他们已经检查了，并且可以保持检查。

sentences.zh: 他们变老了，他们失去了原本拥有的东西，这是悲剧，但主要是他们的问题，他们想把这个问题延伸到那些没有它的人身上。

blocks.html_en:
I called it out a while ago but the largest market to get destroyed by gen AI was never the professional market - it was the gig economy.

The quality of generative AI may not be top-notch, but it presents an easy drop-in replacement for one-off tasks that people previously outsourced to platforms like Fiverr (voiceovers, logo design, clip art, copy editing, translation, etc).

sentences.en: I called it out a while ago but the largest market to get destroyed by gen AI was never the professional market - it was the gig economy.The quality of generative AI may not be top-notch, but it presents an easy drop-in replacement for one-off tasks that people previously outsourced to platforms like Fiverr (voiceovers, logo design, clip art, copy editing, translation, etc).
sentences.zh: 我不久前就说过，但被生成人工智能摧毁的最大市场从来都不是专业市场，而是零工经济。生成人工智能的质量可能不是一流的，但它为人们以前外包给 Fiverr 等平台的一次性任务（配音、标志设计、剪贴画、文案编辑、翻译等）提供了一种简单的替代品。

blocks.html_en:
To me nothing a human makes has the capacity to be as terrible on an existential level as genAI slop.

> something that genuinely looks like a huge improvement to them

Of course they think it's great, or they wouldn't be doing it. That goes for everyone, with anything, ever. And some customers probably think it's great, too. But if you want me as a customer, to whom all those other opinions are completely irrelevant, you'd be better off just putting a turd glazed with boogers. Doesn't matter what it advertises, I'll know a human made this, and look at the product description.

sentences.en: To me nothing a human makes has the capacity to be as terrible on an existential level as genAI slop.> something that genuinely looks like a huge improvement to themOf course they think it's great, or they wouldn't be doing it.
sentences.zh: 对我来说，人类制造的任何东西都没有能力像 genAI 那样在存在层面上如此可怕。 > 一些看起来确实对他们来说是巨大改进的东西当然他们认为这很棒，否则他们不会这样做。

sentences.en: That goes for everyone, with anything, ever.
sentences.zh: 这对每个人、任何事情都适用。

sentences.en: And some customers probably think it's great, too.
sentences.zh: 一些客户可能也认为这很棒。

sentences.en: But if you want me as a customer, to whom all those other opinions are completely irrelevant, you'd be better off just putting a turd glazed with boogers.
sentences.zh: 但如果你想让我成为你的顾客，而对我来说，所有其他的意见都完全无关紧要，那么你最好只放一块涂有鼻屎的粪便。

sentences.en: Doesn't matter what it advertises, I'll know a human made this, and look at the product description.
sentences.zh: 不管它的广告是什么，我都会知道这是一个人做的，看看产品描述。

blocks.html_en:
It's generally bad vibes because honestly, they're ugly. If you're flat broke, are too many non ugly templates out there. Hiring a local person to do a job like this isn't that expensive, either. All else fails, there's a certain charm in an honest diy poster that AI lacks.

If taste is involved, it's even worse vibes. I mean, an AI poster for a cell phone repair shop is whatever. An AI poster for a clothing store? I don't trust their fashion. This could be the deciding factor on if I get Filipino or Chinese (especially cause the food on the menu in the article looks terrible)...

sentences.zh: 这通常是不好的感觉，因为老实说，它们很丑。

sentences.en: If you're flat broke, are too many non ugly templates out there.

blocks.html_en:
It's always been like this - businesses signal that they have piles of money to burn by burning it on expensive advertising. Before AI, you would have been dubious of an ad that was amateurish/clipart/etc. because it shows they can't be bothered spending money on professional designers. Back in the day, I heard that websites used Flash content for this reason too because it was expensive to produce.

I think it would be better if consumers stopped using burning money as a measure of whether to buy something.

sentences.en: It's always been like this - businesses signal that they have piles of money to burn by burning it on expensive advertising.
sentences.zh: 一直都是这样——企业通过在昂贵的广告上烧钱来表明他们有大把的钱可以烧。

sentences.en: Before AI, you would have been dubious of an ad that was amateurish/clipart/etc. because it shows they can't be bothered spending money on professional designers.
sentences.zh: 在人工智能出现之前，你会对业余/剪贴画/等等的广告表示怀疑。因为这表明他们不愿意花钱请专业设计师。

sentences.en: Back in the day, I heard that websites used Flash content for this reason too because it was expensive to produce.I think it would be better if consumers stopped using burning money as a measure of whether to buy something.
sentences.zh: 以前，我听说网站使用 Flash 内容也是因为这个原因，因为制作成本很高。我认为如果消费者不再用烧钱来衡量是否购买某样东西会更好。

blocks.html_en: I get the consumers' ick when I see an AI-generated menu, logo, or sign as much as the next person. Beyond that, there is an argument to be made about the discrepancy between the pictorial depictions of a food item and what you are actually served in real life. I wish something akin to Japan's strict food packaging laws existed for restaurants.

sentences.en: I get the consumers' ick when I see an AI-generated menu, logo, or sign as much as the next person.
sentences.zh: 当我看到人工智能生成的菜单、标志或标志时，我会像其他人一样感到厌恶。

sentences.en: Beyond that, there is an argument to be made about the discrepancy between the pictorial depictions of a food item and what you are actually served in real life.
sentences.zh: 除此之外，关于食物的图片描述与现实生活中实际提供的食物之间的差异也存在争议。

sentences.en: I wish something akin to Japan's strict food packaging laws existed for restaurants.
sentences.zh: 我希望餐馆能有类似于日本严格的食品包装法的规定。

blocks.html_en:
I have a few friends I’d describe as high up in the restaurant industry. They’ll usually be skeptical of a menu with photos unless it’s a mom and pop place that’s been around for decades.

There’s a big reason more expensive restaurants don’t have pictures on their menus, even when they’re the best equipped to ensure your meal matches the picture.

sentences.en: I have a few friends I’d describe as high up in the restaurant industry.
sentences.zh: 我有几个朋友，我认为他们是餐饮业的高层。

sentences.en: They’ll usually be skeptical of a menu with photos unless it’s a mom and pop place that’s been around for decades.There’s a big reason more expensive restaurants don’t have pictures on their menus, even when they’re the best equipped to ensure your meal matches the picture.
sentences.zh: 他们通常会对带有照片的菜单持怀疑态度，除非这是一家已经存在了几十年的夫妻店。更昂贵的餐厅在菜单上没有图片是一个很大的原因，即使他们最有能力确保你的饭菜与图片相符。
