blocks.html_en:
For food in addition to the false advertising it's just going to be the uncanny valley: there's thousands of years of evolutionary motivation to try and assess if food is safe. Any amount of "wrongness" will put people right off.

There's some hot take incoming about highly processed foods, but the reality is that's still designed and made by humans, for humans, and so passes all those initial hurdles like "is this poison or a trap?"

An image model doesn't even have that grounding.

sentences.en: For food in addition to the false advertising it's just going to be the uncanny valley: there's thousands of years of evolutionary motivation to try and assess if food is safe.
sentences.zh: 对于食品来说，除了虚假广告之外，这将是一个恐怖谷：数千年来的进化动机都在尝试评估食品是否安全。

sentences.en: Any amount of "wrongness" will put people right off.There's some hot take incoming about highly processed foods, but the reality is that's still designed and made by humans, for humans, and so passes all those initial hurdles like "is this poison or a trap?"An image model doesn't even have that grounding.
sentences.zh: 任何数量的“错误”都会让人们立刻感到厌烦。关于高度加工食品有一些热门观点，但现实是，这些食品仍然是由人类设计和制造的，为人类服务，因此克服了所有最初的障碍，例如“这是毒药还是陷阱？”图像模型甚至没有这样的基础。

blocks.html_en:
For food in addition to the false advertising it's just going to be the uncanny valley: there's thousands of years of evolutionary motivation to try and assess if food is safe. Any amount of "wrongness" will put people right off.

There's some hot take incoming about highly processed foods, but the reality is that's still designed and made by humans, for humans, and so passes all those initial hurdles like "is this poison or a trap?"

An image model doesn't even have that grounding.

blocks.html_en:
I do think that AI signage/advertising is the new signifier of low-effort, low-skill output and that is how it will be perceived by clientele.

Similar to someone just scrawling a sandwich board sign in chalk (which at this point I would prefer).

Businesses will stand out from a taste perspective by continuing to hire human effort to make recognisable human output.

sentences.en: I do think that AI signage/advertising is the new signifier of low-effort, low-skill output and that is how it will be perceived by clientele.Similar to someone just scrawling a sandwich board sign in chalk (which at this point I would prefer).Businesses will stand out from a taste perspective by continuing to hire human effort to make recognisable human output.
sentences.zh: 我确实认为人工智能标牌/广告是低努力、低技能产出的新标志，这就是客户对它的看法。类似于有人只是用粉笔在三明治板上写字（在这一点上我更喜欢）。通过继续雇用人力来创造可识别的人力产出，企业将从品味的角度脱颖而出。

blocks.html_en: Writing in signs and menus in chalk has been a signal for a high quality restaurant for as long as I can remember. With photos of the food, and/or ring bound menus being the worst.

sentences.zh: 从我记事起，用粉笔书写的招牌和菜单就一直是高品质餐厅的标志。

sentences.en: With photos of the food, and/or ring bound menus being the worst.
sentences.zh: 食物的照片和/或环形菜单是最糟糕的。

blocks.html_en:
I knew someone that actually got a degree in like letterprinting with a minor in menu design. She went from town-to-town creating these beautiful chalk menu designs and lived a pretty modest life.

One day a tattoo parlor asked her to make a sign. She did. They asked if she had ever thought about getting into tattooing. She hadn't. They gave her an apprenticeship.

After completing her apprenticeship, she's now booked so far into advance that she closed her book to the public.

I think about her all the time now, what with software engineering going the way that it is. What lateral like passion could I transition to, as she did?

sentences.en: I knew someone that actually got a degree in like letterprinting with a minor in menu design.
sentences.zh: 我认识一个人，他实际上获得了字母印刷学位，辅修菜单设计。

sentences.en: She went from town-to-town creating these beautiful chalk menu designs and lived a pretty modest life.One day a tattoo parlor asked her to make a sign.
sentences.zh: 她从一个城镇到另一个城镇创作了这些美丽的粉笔菜单设计，过着相当朴素的生活。有一天，一家纹身店要求她做一个标志。

sentences.en: She did.
sentences.zh: 她做到了。

sentences.en: They asked if she had ever thought about getting into tattooing.
sentences.zh: 他们问她是否考虑过纹身。

sentences.en: She hadn't.
sentences.zh: 她没有。

sentences.en: They gave her an apprenticeship.After completing her apprenticeship, she's now booked so far into advance that she closed her book to the public.I think about her all the time now, what with software engineering going the way that it is.
sentences.zh: 他们给了她一个学徒期。完成学徒期后，她现在的预订已经提前了，以至于她不再向公众公开她的书。我现在一直在想她，软件工程发展到现在的样子。

sentences.en: What lateral like passion could I transition to, as she did?
sentences.zh: 我可以像她一样过渡到什么侧面的激情？

sentences.zh: 下个月的这个时候，你可能会在某人的屁股上纹上缓冲区溢出的内容。

blocks.html_en: Photos of the food, at least in the US, started with "immigrant" restaurants. When Thai/Vietnamese/Chinese restaurants started opening up in areas without high concentrations of those people, they'd put photos in the menu so you could see what you were getting since that wouldn't be clear from the names or descriptions in the menu. A lot of these restaurants were cheap, which is why I think you associate it with low-quality restaurants.

sentences.en: Photos of the food, at least in the US, started with "immigrant" restaurants.
sentences.zh: 至少在美国，食物的照片都是从“移民”餐馆开始的。

sentences.en: When Thai/Vietnamese/Chinese restaurants started opening up in areas without high concentrations of those people, they'd put photos in the menu so you could see what you were getting since that wouldn't be clear from the names or descriptions in the menu.
sentences.zh: 当泰国/越南/中国餐馆开始在这些人不高度集中的地区开业时，他们会在菜单中放上照片，这样你就可以看到你点的是什么，因为菜单中的名称或描述并不清楚。

sentences.en: A lot of these restaurants were cheap, which is why I think you associate it with low-quality restaurants.
sentences.zh: 这些餐馆很多都很便宜，这就是为什么我认为你会把它与低质量的餐馆联系起来。

blocks.html_en: U.S. chains like IHOP and Denny's beat the mom-and-pop immigrant restaurants by decades (and they are truly terrible). I wouldn't be surprised if they got the idea from even lower quality chains like McDonald's, but I'm not old enough to know.

sentences.en: U.S. chains like IHOP and Denny's beat the mom-and-pop immigrant restaurants by decades (and they are truly terrible).
sentences.zh: 像 IHOP 和 Denny's 这样的美国连锁店比夫妻移民餐馆领先了几十年（而且它们确实很糟糕）。

sentences.en: I wouldn't be surprised if they got the idea from even lower quality chains like McDonald's, but I'm not old enough to know.
sentences.zh: 如果他们从像麦当劳这样质量较低的连锁店那里得到这个想法，我不会感到惊讶，但我还不够老，不知道。

blocks.html_en: U.S. chains like IHOP and Denny's beat the mom-and-pop immigrant restaurants by decades (and they are truly terrible). I wouldn't be surprised if they got the idea from even lower quality chains like McDonald's, but I'm not old enough to know.

sentences.en: U.S. chains like IHOP and Denny's beat the mom-and-pop immigrant restaurants by decades (and they are truly terrible).
sentences.zh: 像 IHOP 和 Denny's 这样的美国连锁店比夫妻移民餐馆领先了几十年（而且它们确实很糟糕）。

sentences.en: I wouldn't be surprised if they got the idea from even lower quality chains like McDonald's, but I'm not old enough to know.

blocks.html_en:
Indeed. It seems the parent comment has accidentally made exactly the opposite point than they intended.

The difference between scrawl on a chalk board and a glossy menu with photos is not really the visual effect, it's the amount of effort and the virtue signalling - "my food is so amazing I don't need to make more effort than this".

But that applies to AI menus too. "Why are these horrible AI slop photos on your menu?" "Because I'm too focused on food to hand create a perfect American Psycho minimalist menu"

sentences.en: Indeed.
sentences.zh: 的确。

sentences.en: It seems the parent comment has accidentally made exactly the opposite point than they intended.The difference between scrawl on a chalk board and a glossy menu with photos is not really the visual effect, it's the amount of effort and the virtue signalling - "my food is so amazing I don't need to make more effort than this".But that applies to AI menus too.
sentences.zh: 看来家长的评论无意中提出了与他们的意图完全相反的观点。黑板上的潦草涂鸦和带有照片的光面菜单之间的区别并不是真正的视觉效果，而是努力的程度和美德信号——“我的食物太棒了，我不需要比这付出更多的努力”。但这也适用于人工智能菜单。

sentences.en: "Why are these horrible AI slop photos on your menu?"
sentences.zh: “为什么这些可怕的人工智能照片会出现在你的菜单上？”

sentences.en: "Because I'm too focused on food to hand create a perfect American Psycho minimalist menu"
sentences.zh: “因为我太专注于食物而无法亲手制作完美的《美国精神病人》极简主义菜单”

blocks.html_en:
> Similar to someone just scrawling a sandwich board sign in chalk

As opposed to what? This is usually a sign of quality. If it’s in chalk it’s because it changes as the restaurant experiments and tries new things.

sentences.en: > Similar to someone just scrawling a sandwich board sign in chalkAs opposed to what?
sentences.zh: > 类似于有人用粉笔在三明治板上乱写乱画，而不是什么？

sentences.en: This is usually a sign of quality.
sentences.zh: 这通常是质量的标志。

sentences.en: If it’s in chalk it’s because it changes as the restaurant experiments and tries new things.
sentences.zh: 如果它是用粉笔写的，那是因为它随着餐厅的实验和尝试新事物而改变。

blocks.html_en: Yeah, there’s a great bar I sometimes go to where the taplist is a chalkboard because of their ~20 taps they’re typically changing at least one per day to a new keg.

blocks.html_en:
GPT-Image has that horrible deep-fried look to it. Can't draw anything without looking like it's got grit thrown over the whole image. Nano Banana is much, much better here, it's often hard to tell if it's fake.

Apart from that image generation is just the wrong idea for flyers since the resolution they put out is never enough for 300 DPI, the image always looks low-quality, the edges of the text are blurry and so on. Also the fact that the images in the picture look nothing like the real thing is unethical.

I think you could have an AI system that makes menus correctly: the restaurant would take (amateur) pictures of all their menu items, give the system the names, descriptions, prices and so on, then the agent could use Nano Banana or something like that to give the images consistent lighting and angles, use a different model to remove the backgrounds and then write a menu in the desired sizes using HTML. That way the text could be perfect, the images could look like what you actually get.

sentences.en: GPT-Image has that horrible deep-fried look to it.
sentences.zh: GPT-Image 具有可怕的油炸外观。

sentences.en: Can't draw anything without looking like it's got grit thrown over the whole image.
sentences.zh: 无法画出任何东西，看起来整个图像都被沙子覆盖了。

sentences.en: Nano Banana is much, much better here, it's often hard to tell if it's fake.Apart from that image generation is just the wrong idea for flyers since the resolution they put out is never enough for 300 DPI, the image always looks low-quality, the edges of the text are blurry and so on.
sentences.zh: Nano Banana 在这里要好得多，通常很难辨别它是否是假的。除了图像生成对于传单来说是错误的想法，因为他们提供的分辨率永远不足以达到 300 DPI，图像总是看起来质量低下，文本边缘模糊等等。

sentences.en: Also the fact that the images in the picture look nothing like the real thing is unethical.I think you could have an AI system that makes menus correctly: the restaurant would take (amateur) pictures of all their menu items, give the system the names, descriptions, prices and so on, then the agent could use Nano Banana or something like that to give the images consistent lighting and angles, use a different model to remove the backgrounds and then write a menu in the desired sizes using HTML.
sentences.zh: 另外，图片中的图像看起来与真实的东西完全不同，这是不道德的。我认为你可以拥有一个正确制作菜单的人工智能系统：餐厅会为所有菜单项拍摄（业余）照片，为系统提供名称、描述、价格等，然后代理可以使用 Nano Banana 或类似的东西来为图像提供一致的照明和角度，使用不同的模型来删除背景，然后使用 HTML 编写所需大小的菜单。

sentences.en: That way the text could be perfect, the images could look like what you actually get.
sentences.zh: 这样文本就可以是完美的，图像就可以看起来像您实际得到的一样。

blocks.html_en:
I think 5 years ago, we'd just have thought this was a "badly designed menu" and moved on, but now AI has taught us to feel a deep sense of disappointment with it's particular blend of poor personality-less design.

I was looking at the documentation for cruft the other day and it's mascot is absolutely glorious. Not because it displays any semblance of "good taste", but just because it is the polar opposite of AI generated blandness[0].

Who knows - maybe in the future we'll start embracing signals of human amateurishness and ms-paint created menus filled with comic sans will take off.

[0] https://cruft.github.io/cruft/

sentences.en: I think 5 years ago, we'd just have thought this was a "badly designed menu" and moved on, but now AI has taught us to feel a deep sense of disappointment with it's particular blend of poor personality-less design.I was looking at the documentation for cruft the other day and it's mascot is absolutely glorious.
sentences.zh: 我想五年前，我们只是认为这是一个“设计糟糕的菜单”并继续前进，但现在人工智能已经教会我们对它特别融合了糟糕的缺乏个性的设计感到深深的失望。前几天我在看 cruft 的文档，它的吉祥物绝对是辉煌的。

sentences.en: Not because it displays any semblance of "good taste", but just because it is the polar opposite of AI generated blandness[0].Who knows - maybe in the future we'll start embracing signals of human amateurishness and ms-paint created menus filled with comic sans will take off.[0] https://cruft.github.io/cruft/
sentences.zh: 并不是因为它表现出任何“好品味”，而是因为它与人工智能生成的平淡性截然相反[0]。谁知道呢——也许将来我们会开始接受人类业余的信号，而 ms-paint 创建的充满漫画无衬线的菜单将会起飞。[0] https://cruft.github.io/cruft/

blocks.html_en: looks like every kebab shop menu near me. All offerings crudely cut/pasted/mashed together. 5 near me literally have the same menu with different prices and a colour theme to match their (usually terrible, 3-D font effect from 90's era Corel Draw) Logo. And they were like that BEFORE GenAI got popular!

sentences.en: looks like every kebab shop menu near me.
sentences.zh: 看起来像我附近每家烤肉店的菜单。

sentences.en: 5 near me literally have the same menu with different prices and a colour theme to match their (usually terrible, 3-D font effect from 90's era Corel Draw) Logo.

sentences.en: And they were like that BEFORE GenAI got popular!
sentences.zh: 在 GenAI 流行之前他们就是这样的！

blocks.html_en: I always feel my hunger disappearing when I see ai generated images of food. I lose all appetite. The pictures never look appetizing. Sometimes I even feel like it is illegal to look at them, as if I look at something so unnatural my body warns me to look away.

sentences.en: I always feel my hunger disappearing when I see ai generated images of food.
sentences.zh: 当我看到人工智能生成的食物图像时，我总是感觉饥饿感消失了。

sentences.en: I lose all appetite.
sentences.zh: 我失去了所有的胃口。

sentences.en: The pictures never look appetizing.
sentences.zh: 这些照片看起来从来都没有胃口。

sentences.en: Sometimes I even feel like it is illegal to look at them, as if I look at something so unnatural my body warns me to look away.
sentences.zh: 有时我什至觉得看它们是违法的，就好像我看着一些不自然的东西，我的身体警告我把目光移开。

blocks.html_en:
I've seen some extremely unnatural ones, like börek that looks overstuffed or Afghan Kabuli Palau with what looked like chocolate squares instead of meat chunks! It feels like looking at sci-fi horror.

The craziest offense I have ever seen was (in Germany) an add for a Turkish Kokoreç sandwich where they had a picture of the entire spit of the kokereç being cooked, unchopped, sitting in a bun!!

sentences.en: I've seen some extremely unnatural ones, like börek that looks overstuffed or Afghan Kabuli Palau with what looked like chocolate squares instead of meat chunks!
sentences.zh: 我见过一些极其不自然的东西，比如看起来塞满了东西的 börek 或阿富汗喀布尔帕劳的东西看起来像巧克力块而不是肉块！

sentences.en: It feels like looking at sci-fi horror.The craziest offense I have ever seen was (in Germany) an add for a Turkish Kokoreç sandwich where they had a picture of the entire spit of the kokereç being cooked, unchopped, sitting in a bun!!
sentences.zh: 感觉就像在看科幻恐怖片。我见过的最疯狂的冒犯是（在德国）土耳其 Kokoreç 三明治的添加，他们有一张 kokereç 整个吐口被煮熟的照片，未切碎，坐在一个小圆面包里！

blocks.html_en: There’s a lot of times I see people reaching for AI instead of taking a photograph, this is super egregious when it’s meant as a reference, because the AI generated image won’t necessarily look like the real thing. I’ve seen it used in reference documents about animal management/veterinary reference meant for farmers, and the generated cows, horses and so on are just worse than the original version that was done with photos, they may look more “clean”, but farm life isn’t meant to be clean and it also lost the veterinarian that was shown in the shots manipulating the animal and properly indicating with hands where to pay attention in the animal.

sentences.en: There’s a lot of times I see people reaching for AI instead of taking a photograph, this is super egregious when it’s meant as a reference, because the AI generated image won’t necessarily look like the real thing.
sentences.zh: 很多时候我看到人们使用人工智能而不是拍照，当它作为参考时，这是非常令人震惊的，因为人工智能生成的图像不一定看起来像真实的东西。

sentences.en: I’ve seen it used in reference documents about animal management/veterinary reference meant for farmers, and the generated cows, horses and so on are just worse than the original version that was done with photos, they may look more “clean”, but farm life isn’t meant to be clean and it also lost the veterinarian that was shown in the shots manipulating the animal and properly indicating with hands where to pay attention in the animal.
sentences.zh: 我在关于动物管理/兽医参考的参考文件中看到过它，供农民参考，生成的牛、马等只是比用照片完成的原始版本更糟糕，它们可能看起来更“干净”，但农场生活并不意味着干净，它也失去了镜头中操纵动物并正确用手指示动物中需要注意的地方的兽医。

blocks.html_en: I don’t understand why people use this for _illustrated menus_, of all things. The uncanny valley is particularly offputting in the context of _food_, and ai-generated food images often look very wrong.

sentences.en: I don’t understand why people use this for _illustrated menus_, of all things.
sentences.zh: 我不明白为什么人们用它来做_图解菜单_。

sentences.en: The uncanny valley is particularly offputting in the context of _food_, and ai-generated food images often look very wrong.
sentences.zh: 在“食物”的背景下，恐怖谷尤其令人反感，而人工智能生成的食物图像通常看起来非常错误。

blocks.html_en:
Because it’s cheaper, whether they got it through fiverr, did it themselves, or might even think it looks good? I don’t think a place that signs off on plating its dishes that way is thinking deeply about these kinds of things.

The irony is that you could very well get AI to make a menu that looks far better, but whoever did this is clearly putting about as much care into it as the restaurant puts into plate presentation.

It doesn’t really matter, a Philippine Hawaiian restaurant is probably not long for this world anyways. Most restaurants aren’t.

sentences.en: Because it’s cheaper, whether they got it through fiverr, did it themselves, or might even think it looks good?
sentences.zh: 因为它更便宜，无论他们是通过 Fiverr 购买的，还是自己做的，或者甚至可能认为它看起来不错？

sentences.en: I don’t think a place that signs off on plating its dishes that way is thinking deeply about these kinds of things.The irony is that you could very well get AI to make a menu that looks far better, but whoever did this is clearly putting about as much care into it as the restaurant puts into plate presentation.It doesn’t really matter, a Philippine Hawaiian restaurant is probably not long for this world anyways.
sentences.zh: 我不认为以这种方式摆盘的餐厅会深入思考这些事情。讽刺的是，你完全可以让人工智能制作出看起来更好的菜单，但无论是谁做的，显然都像餐厅在摆盘上一样用心。这并不重要，无论如何，菲律宾夏威夷餐厅可能不会在这个世界上存在太久。

sentences.en: Most restaurants aren’t.
sentences.zh: 大多数餐馆都没有。

blocks.html_en:
Low effort products will be used by low effort companies. In the case of restaurants, this might not necessarily be bad - I’ve been to amazing taco/burrito shops w abysmal menus. But if I went to a “high end” place with an AI menu and instagram decor - bad, bad sign.

I genuinely think AI companies need to worry more about their brand. Just through how their products are used, they are perceived as a marker for low quality.

sentences.en: Low effort products will be used by low effort companies.
sentences.zh: 省力产品将由省力公司使用。

sentences.en: In the case of restaurants, this might not necessarily be bad - I’ve been to amazing taco/burrito shops w abysmal menus.
sentences.zh: 就餐馆而言，这可能并不一定是坏事——我去过很棒的炸玉米饼/墨西哥卷饼店，但菜单很糟糕。

sentences.en: But if I went to a “high end” place with an AI menu and instagram decor - bad, bad sign.I genuinely think AI companies need to worry more about their brand.
sentences.zh: 但如果我去一个有人工智能菜单和 Instagram 装饰的“高端”地方——很糟糕的迹象。我真的认为人工智能公司需要更多地担心他们的品牌。

sentences.en: Just through how their products are used, they are perceived as a marker for low quality.
sentences.zh: 仅仅通过他们产品的使用方式，他们就被视为低质量的标志。

blocks.html_en:
Low effort products will be used by low effort companies. In the case of restaurants, this might not necessarily be bad - I’ve been to amazing taco/burrito shops w abysmal menus. But if I went to a “high end” place with an AI menu and instagram decor - bad, bad sign.

I genuinely think AI companies need to worry more about their brand. Just through how their products are used, they are perceived as a marker for low quality.

blocks.html_en: Anthropic has been running the exact same video ad in my youtube feed for months showing a guy making the tackiest "impossible" coffee table hanging from chains that I wouldn't even bother clicking on if it were a thumbnail from a favorite woodworker. I'd think if ChatGPT were such a great creative tool they could use it to create more than one ad....

sentences.en: Anthropic has been running the exact same video ad in my youtube feed for months showing a guy making the tackiest "impossible" coffee table hanging from chains that I wouldn't even bother clicking on if it were a thumbnail from a favorite woodworker.
sentences.zh: 几个月来，Anthropic 一直在我的 YouTube feed 上播放完全相同的视频广告，展示一个人制作的最俗气的“不可能”的咖啡桌，挂在链子上，如果它是来自最喜欢的木工的缩略图，我什至懒得点击。

sentences.en: I'd think if ChatGPT were such a great creative tool they could use it to create more than one ad....
sentences.zh: 我认为如果 ChatGPT 是一个如此出色的创意工具，他们可以用它来制作多个广告......

blocks.html_en:
> But if I went to a “high end” place with an AI menu and instagram decor - bad, bad sign.

Sometimes I wonder if this will settle into its own form of kitsch over the years, and whether people will someday try to emulate it (with a wink and a nod).

sentences.en: > But if I went to a “high end” place with an AI menu and instagram decor - bad, bad sign.Sometimes I wonder if this will settle into its own form of kitsch over the years, and whether people will someday try to emulate it (with a wink and a nod).

blocks.html_en: I live in Brazil, and on our delivery apps (like iFood), AI photos have become a plague. It's so bad seeing pictures with that distinct GPT look. You just don't know what your rice and beans will look like.

sentences.en: I live in Brazil, and on our delivery apps (like iFood), AI photos have become a plague.
sentences.zh: 我住在巴西，在我们的外卖应用程序（如 iFood）上，人工智能照片已经成为一种瘟疫。

sentences.en: It's so bad seeing pictures with that distinct GPT look.
sentences.zh: 看到具有独特 GPT 外观的图片真是太糟糕了。

sentences.en: You just don't know what your rice and beans will look like.
sentences.zh: 你只是不知道你的大米和豆子会是什么样子。

blocks.html_en:
In the US, some of those apps were generating gross nonsense like showing an image of a pizza with a sweet pie filling (because the menu said “pizza pie”).

I don’t get how AI images like this don’t open them up to false advertising lawsuits

sentences.en: In the US, some of those apps were generating gross nonsense like showing an image of a pizza with a sweet pie filling (because the menu said “pizza pie”).I don’t get how AI images like this don’t open them up to false advertising lawsuits
sentences.zh: 在美国，其中一些应用程序产生了严重的废话，例如显示带有甜馅饼馅的披萨的图像（因为菜单上写着“披萨饼”）。我不明白这样的人工智能图像如何不会让它们面临虚假广告诉讼

blocks.html_en:
Clocked this a while ago, crazy how the smell of the same model they're using is so distinct that entire areas of town are starting to look homogenous.

Someones definitely going to write a think piece about how this erases aesthetic identity of cities and cultures.

Looks like absolute ass too, easily beaten even by something designed by someone completely naive to design/art, good sign of a place to avoid.

sentences.en: Clocked this a while ago, crazy how the smell of the same model they're using is so distinct that entire areas of town are starting to look homogenous.Someones definitely going to write a think piece about how this erases aesthetic identity of cities and cultures.Looks like absolute ass too, easily beaten even by something designed by someone completely naive to design/art, good sign of a place to avoid.
sentences.zh: 不久前就注意到了这一点，疯狂的是，他们使用的同一个模型的气味如此明显，以至于整个城镇区域开始看起来同质化。肯定有人会写一篇关于这如何消除城市和文化的审美认同的思考文章。看起来也绝对是屁股，即使是由对设计/艺术完全天真的人设计的东西也很容易打败，这是一个应该避免的地方的好兆头。

blocks.html_en:
But consider how, in just a short few years, tools will have changed so much that even achieving this specific look will not even be possible (everything is in the cloud and normal users can’t even run them locally)

We’ll inevitably end up feeling nostalgic for these mid-2020s AI slop menus, much like how graphic design of the 90s and 2000s is revered (see other comments on this post) even while during its making was regarded as garish

sentences.en: But consider how, in just a short few years, tools will have changed so much that even achieving this specific look will not even be possible (everything is in the cloud and normal users can’t even run them locally)We’ll inevitably end up feeling nostalgic for these mid-2020s AI slop menus, much like how graphic design of the 90s and 2000s is revered (see other comments on this post) even while during its making was regarded as garish
sentences.zh: 但考虑一下，在短短几年内，工具将发生如此之大的变化，甚至实现这种特定的外观也是不可能的（一切都在云端，普通用户甚至无法在本地运行它们）我们将不可避免地对这些 2020 年代中期的 AI 倾斜菜单感到怀旧，就像 90 年代和 2000 年代的图形设计受到推崇一样（请参阅本文中的其他评论），即使在其制作过程中被认为是花哨的

blocks.html_en:
My personal opinion is that these days most people simply wouldn’t be able to tell that the photos are AI generated, and even if they could, they wouldn’t care or have any strong thoughts about it. I don’t think my parents or any adults aged 50+ in my family have spent any time at all playing around with AI image generation tools or comparing AI-generated images to non-AI-generated images to be able to have any opinions about these things.

So until more people frequenting restaurants care it’s a relatively low risk and low cost way to update your menu.

sentences.en: My personal opinion is that these days most people simply wouldn’t be able to tell that the photos are AI generated, and even if they could, they wouldn’t care or have any strong thoughts about it.
sentences.zh: 我个人的观点是，如今大多数人根本无法辨别这些照片是人工智能生成的，即使他们可以，他们也不会关心或对此有任何强烈的想法。

sentences.en: I don’t think my parents or any adults aged 50+ in my family have spent any time at all playing around with AI image generation tools or comparing AI-generated images to non-AI-generated images to be able to have any opinions about these things.So until more people frequenting restaurants care it’s a relatively low risk and low cost way to update your menu.
sentences.zh: 我认为我的父母或家里任何 50 岁以上的成年人都没有花任何时间玩弄人工智能图像生成工具，或者将人工智能生成的图像与非人工智能生成的图像进行比较，以便能够对这些事情有任何意见。因此，在更多经常光顾餐馆的人关心之前，更新菜单是一种相对低风险和低成本的方式。

blocks.html_en: Yeah... The "Authentic Indian" curry house in my town replaced everything with AI. So the food really doesn't look like whatever they are serving. This was an issue always with plastic / pro photographed food, but now it costs nothing and it immediately jumps out as definitely not authentic.

sentences.en: Yeah...
sentences.zh: 是的...

sentences.en: The "Authentic Indian" curry house in my town replaced everything with AI.
sentences.zh: 我镇上的“正宗印度”咖喱屋用人工智能取代了一切。

sentences.en: So the food really doesn't look like whatever they are serving.
sentences.zh: 所以食物看起来真的不像他们提供的那样。

sentences.en: This was an issue always with plastic / pro photographed food, but now it costs nothing and it immediately jumps out as definitely not authentic.
sentences.zh: 对于塑料/专业拍摄的食物来说，这一直是一个问题，但现在它不需要任何成本，而且它立即跳出来，因为它绝对不是正宗的。

blocks.html_en:
Recently I was shopping for curtains. Even though the shop I found has great reviews and a physical showroom with a real person, I took my business elsewhere when the web looks like this: https://en.vorhangbox.ch/curtain-home-consultation/ .

Why would you do this to your business? I always found generic photobank stuff to be the worst, but apparently the bar can go lower.

sentences.en: Recently I was shopping for curtains.
sentences.zh: 最近去买窗帘。

sentences.en: Even though the shop I found has great reviews and a physical showroom with a real person, I took my business elsewhere when the web looks like this: https://en.vorhangbox.ch/curtain-home-consultation/ .Why would you do this to your business?
sentences.zh: 尽管我发现的商店有很好的评论，并且有一个真人的实体陈列室，但当网络看起来像这样时，我把我的生意转移到了其他地方：https://en.vorhangbox.ch/curtain-home-consultation/。你为什么要对你的生意这样做？

sentences.en: I always found generic photobank stuff to be the worst, but apparently the bar can go lower.
sentences.zh: 我总是发现通用照片库的东西是最糟糕的，但显然标准可以更低。

blocks.html_en:
So how did the website of the place you moved to looks like?

I don't understand what is so wrong with that website. I don't love it, I don't have it either. If you were looking for a business that makes websites and you don't like their website I would understand, but this is not the case here.

sentences.zh: 那么你搬到的地方的网站看起来怎么样？我不明白那个网站有什么问题。

sentences.en: I don't love it, I don't have it either.
sentences.zh: 我不爱它，我也没有它。

sentences.en: If you were looking for a business that makes websites and you don't like their website I would understand, but this is not the case here.
sentences.zh: 如果您正在寻找一家制作网站的企业并且您不喜欢他们的网站，我会理解，但这里的情况并非如此。

blocks.html_en:
> Why would you do this to your business?

Maybe they hired someone to get a website and they were sold this AI slop?

I've seen a lot of SMB in my area get terrible AI slop websites recently. After looking at the footer some "web agency" sold them. They claim to sell "hand crafted, no framework" websites, but it looks super terrible.

sentences.zh: > 你为什么要对你的企业这样做？也许他们雇了人来建立一个网站，然后他们被卖了这个人工智能垃圾？我最近看到我所在地区的很多中小型企业都得到了糟糕的人工智能垃圾网站。

sentences.zh: 在查看页脚后，一些“网络代理”将其出售。

sentences.en: They claim to sell "hand crafted, no framework" websites, but it looks super terrible.
sentences.zh: 他们声称出售“手工制作、无框架”的网站，但看起来超级糟糕。

blocks.html_en:
I'm starting to see more and more generated images of food at restaurants where I live, but I'm happy to say that its still somewhat rare.

My partner and I usually just give a one star review each on Google when we pass by a place like this and then carry on.

sentences.en: I'm starting to see more and more generated images of food at restaurants where I live, but I'm happy to say that its still somewhat rare.My partner and I usually just give a one star review each on Google when we pass by a place like this and then carry on.
sentences.zh: 我开始在我居住的餐厅看到越来越多的生成的食物图像，但我很高兴地说这仍然有点罕见。当我们经过这样的地方时，我和我的伴侣通常只会在谷歌上给出一星评价，然后继续前进。

blocks.html_en: That's the definition of judging a book by its cover. Maybe they don't care about spending money on design and just want to focus on the food they serve, you'll never know. I would also probably be less inclined to enter a restaurant if I saw generated images advertising the food as opposed to real ones. But I would never ever dare put a one star review without even having been a customer there in the first place. I would remove 2 stars at most from a 5 stars review, if the food and service was impeccable but they ruined the settings with AI.

sentences.en: That's the definition of judging a book by its cover.
sentences.zh: 这就是以貌取人的定义。

sentences.en: Maybe they don't care about spending money on design and just want to focus on the food they serve, you'll never know.
sentences.zh: 也许他们不在乎在设计上花钱，只想专注于他们提供的食物，你永远不会知道。

sentences.en: I would also probably be less inclined to enter a restaurant if I saw generated images advertising the food as opposed to real ones.
sentences.zh: 如果我看到生成的食品广告图像而不是真实的图像，我也可能不太愿意进入餐厅。

sentences.en: But I would never ever dare put a one star review without even having been a customer there in the first place.
sentences.zh: 但我从来不敢在没有成为那里的顾客的情况下发表一星评论。

sentences.en: I would remove 2 stars at most from a 5 stars review, if the food and service was impeccable but they ruined the settings with AI.
sentences.zh: 如果食物和服务无可挑剔，但他们用人工智能破坏了设置，我最多会从 5 星评论中删除 2 星。

sentences.en: I would also probably be less inclined to enter a restaurant if I saw generated images advertising the food as opposed to real ones.
sentences.zh: 如果我看到生成的食品广告图像而不是真实的图像，我也可能不太愿意进入餐厅。

sentences.en: But I would never ever dare put a one star review without even having been a customer there in the first place.
sentences.zh: 但我从来不敢在没有成为那里的顾客的情况下发表一星评论。

sentences.en: I would remove 2 stars at most from a 5 stars review, if the food and service was impeccable but they ruined the settings with AI.
sentences.zh: 如果食物和服务无可挑剔，但他们用人工智能破坏了设置，我最多会从 5 星评论中删除 2 星。

blocks.html_en:
> That's the definition of judging a book by its cover.

I’m not sure whether I disagree that a menu is a book or whether judging a book by its cover is a bad thing in this instance.

A menu has to list certain allergens, but not all. That would be inconvenient. With pictures however, people with allergies can more efficiently rule out or order dishes based on what they see.

Generated images breaks with this. I’ve seen multiple places not add certain ingredients to their images that would be there in a photo. I think this is irresponsible. You are free to disagree though, but you need a proper argument if you want to convince us.

sentences.en: > That's the definition of judging a book by its cover.I’m not sure whether I disagree that a menu is a book or whether judging a book by its cover is a bad thing in this instance.A menu has to list certain allergens, but not all.
sentences.zh: > 这就是通过封面判断一本书的定义。我不确定我是否不同意菜单是一本书，或者在这种情况下通过封面判断一本书是否是一件坏事。菜单必须列出某些过敏原，但不是全部。

sentences.en: That would be inconvenient.
sentences.zh: 那会很不方便。

sentences.en: With pictures however, people with allergies can more efficiently rule out or order dishes based on what they see.Generated images breaks with this.
sentences.zh: 然而，有了图片，过敏的人就可以根据他们所看到的内容更有效地排除或点菜。生成的图像打破了这一点。

sentences.en: I’ve seen multiple places not add certain ingredients to their images that would be there in a photo.
sentences.zh: 我见过很多地方没有在照片中添加某些成分到他们的图像中。

sentences.en: I think this is irresponsible.
sentences.zh: 我认为这是不负责任的。

sentences.en: You are free to disagree though, but you need a proper argument if you want to convince us.
sentences.zh: 你可以自由地提出不同意见，但如果你想说服我们，你需要一个适当的论据。

blocks.html_en:
Just so I have this clear, you rate a restaurant badly based on the pictures of the food and not the taste/flavor/etc?

These places are doing their best to compete in a cutthroat environment with often razor-thin margins but you are willing to write them off and actively try to harm them for reaching for a tool to try and meet consumer's ever-increasing expectations?

Just like in the native vs electron debate it's not "Beautiful hand-crafted native app" or "Electron wasteful trash", it's "Electron or nothing".

I don't begrudge people at all for reaching for LLM/AI to help cover over what they are incapable of doing or unable to pay other people for. And while "anyone can take a picture" taking good food photography is difficult.

sentences.en: Just so I have this clear, you rate a restaurant badly based on the pictures of the food and not the taste/flavor/etc?These places are doing their best to compete in a cutthroat environment with often razor-thin margins but you are willing to write them off and actively try to harm them for reaching for a tool to try and meet consumer's ever-increasing expectations?Just like in the native vs electron debate it's not "Beautiful hand-crafted native app" or "Electron wasteful trash", it's "Electron or nothing".I don't begrudge people at all for reaching for LLM/AI to help cover over what they are incapable of doing or unable to pay other people for.
sentences.zh: 我想清楚的是，您根据食物的图片而不是味道/风味/等对一家餐厅进行了糟糕的评价？这些地方正在尽最大努力在利润微薄的残酷环境中竞争，但您愿意注销它们并积极尝试伤害他们，因为他们试图找到一种工具来尝试满足消费者不断增长的期望？就像在本机与电子辩论中一样，它不是“美丽的手工制作的本机应用程序”或“电子应用程序”浪费垃圾”，这是“电子或什么都没有”。我一点也不嫉妒人们寻求法学硕士/人工智能来帮助掩盖他们无能力做或无法付钱给其他人的事情。

sentences.en: And while "anyone can take a picture" taking good food photography is difficult.
sentences.zh: 虽然“任何人都可以拍照”，但拍摄精美的美食照片却很困难。

blocks.html_en:
> Just like in the native vs electron debate it's not "Beautiful hand-crafted native app" or "Electron wasteful trash", it's "Electron or nothing".

The overwhelming majority of the total uses of Electron apps are going to be the same pile of apps from various megacorps that could fund native apps with change from their couch cushions.

e.g. the sum total of Electron apps I use are Teams (I guess not quite Electron now, but close enough)/Slack/Discord/Android Messages/VS Code.

sentences.en: > Just like in the native vs electron debate it's not "Beautiful hand-crafted native app" or "Electron wasteful trash", it's "Electron or nothing".The overwhelming majority of the total uses of Electron apps are going to be the same pile of apps from various megacorps that could fund native apps with change from their couch cushions.e.g. the sum total of Electron apps I use are Teams (I guess not quite Electron now, but close enough)/Slack/Discord/Android Messages/VS Code.
sentences.zh: > 就像在原生与电子辩论中一样，它不是“漂亮的手工制作的原生应用程序”或“电子浪费的垃圾”，而是“电子或什么都没有”。电子应用程序的总使用量中的绝大多数将是来自各个巨型企业的同一堆应用程序，这些应用程序可以用沙发垫上的零钱为原生应用程序提供资金。我使用的 Electron 应用程序总数是 Teams（我想现在不完全是 Electron，但足够接近）/Slack/Discord/Android Messages/VS Code。

blocks.html_en:
He's giving the restaurant feedback that this is not what the customer wants, in the only meaningful way to provide feedback.

> Electron or nothing

"Nothing" is an open space for someone to provide a better solution so often better in the long term than a bad implementation.

blocks.html_en:
It may require effort to take a nice photo of a dish, but at least its accurate.

An AI generated burger has very little resemblance of what the restaurant is capable of producing.

blocks.html_en:
They make and sell food in an age where literally everyone has a high quality camera in their pocket.

In their core business they're not going to show an actual picture of the actual product they intend to provide?

blocks.html_en:
We always expect what was promised to be different than what was delivered to some degree, but I'm not sure restaurants have fully weighed the expectations-vs-reality backlash this may cause... uncannily-wrong details aside for a sec, the AI pics always set michelin-level presentation expectations. The final promise-vs-plate pics in this post were pretty dramatic.

There's always a class of restaurants where they don't care about repeat visitors. Think the cafe in front of a major tourist attraction: every day brings a new busload of 1-time patrons, those will always want the michelin-level pics to bait the 1-time foot-traffic in. The danger is the neighborhood mom-and-pops will start using the same trick without understanding they need repeat customers, and repeat disappointment kills a repeat audience.

But who knows... people with a TV budget have been using glue in their cheesy pizza commercials, soap in their beer glass foam, and motor oil on their pancake stacks for a long time -- https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9k7PJoNAXkk -- maybe this is just equalizing the enshittified food playing field.

sentences.zh: 暂且不谈那些离奇错误的细节，人工智能图片总是设定米其林级别的展示期望。

blocks.html_en: Different strokes for different folks, I guess. Where I live photos on a menu are mostly used for lower tier restaurants. It's not a good sign.

sentences.en: Different strokes for different folks, I guess.
sentences.zh: 我想不同的人有不同的笔触。

sentences.en: Where I live photos on a menu are mostly used for lower tier restaurants.
sentences.zh: 我住的地方菜单上的照片主要用于低层餐厅。

sentences.en: It's not a good sign.
sentences.zh: 这不是一个好兆头。

blocks.html_en:
We've seen several new restaurants crop up on a busy restaurant street near us, and they all have AI generated logos and food images. All are bad, some are comically bad, some are just false advertising.

So far none seem to be gaining traction.

To me it just sets such a low bar for effort on such a basic part of the experience that I assume the rest of the experience and the food is similar trash.

sentences.en: We've seen several new restaurants crop up on a busy restaurant street near us, and they all have AI generated logos and food images.
sentences.zh: 我们看到附近一条繁忙的餐厅街上出现了几家新餐厅，它们都有人工智能生成的徽标和食物图像。

sentences.zh: 一切都很糟糕，有些糟糕得可笑，有些只是虚假广告。到目前为止，似乎没有一个获得了关注。对我来说，它只是在体验的基本部分上设置了一个很低的标准，我认为其余的体验和食物都是类似的垃圾。

blocks.html_en: These are absolutely consuming Taipei over the last few months. This spring I saw an AI-generated menu for the first time, and then it was like overnight that now on basically every street there's at least one restaurant that has an AI menu, sign, or window dressing. It looks awful and I can't understand how anyone thinks it's a good idea. Noodles give me skin-crawling ants feeling. Chopsticks pick up tofu without pinching it, as if it's drawn magnetically. Spoons dip sauce from bowls that are smaller than the spoon.

sentences.en: These are absolutely consuming Taipei over the last few months.
sentences.zh: 在过去的几个月里，这些绝对消耗了台北的精力。

sentences.en: This spring I saw an AI-generated menu for the first time, and then it was like overnight that now on basically every street there's at least one restaurant that has an AI menu, sign, or window dressing.
sentences.zh: 今年春天，我第一次看到人工智能生成的菜单，然后就像一夜之间，现在基本上每条街道上都至少有一家餐厅有人工智能菜单、招牌或橱窗装饰。

sentences.en: It looks awful and I can't understand how anyone thinks it's a good idea.
sentences.zh: 它看起来很糟糕，我不明白为什么有人认为这是一个好主意。

sentences.en: Noodles give me skin-crawling ants feeling.
sentences.zh: 面条给我一种蚂蚁在皮肤上爬行的感觉。

sentences.en: Chopsticks pick up tofu without pinching it, as if it's drawn magnetically.
sentences.zh: 筷子夹起豆腐，却没有捏住，就像是被磁力吸引一样。

sentences.en: Spoons dip sauce from bowls that are smaller than the spoon.
sentences.zh: 勺子从比勺子小的碗里蘸酱。

blocks.html_en:
I've come around to the other side of this in a way. I used to think "wow they don't care at all" the same way I previously looked at menus clearly made in MS Word: The place will probably be cheap and they don't care about having fancy menu design.

From there, it's two possibilities:

-They care about the food at the expensive of design and it's going to be cheap and great (good) -They care about the food as much as the design and it's going to suck (bad)

This switch happened after a new place opened in my neighborhood that had AI signage and is now my favorite lunch spot.

sentences.en: I've come around to the other side of this in a way.
sentences.zh: 我在某种程度上已经走到了另一边。

sentences.en: I used to think "wow they don't care at all" the same way I previously looked at menus clearly made in MS Word: The place will probably be cheap and they don't care about having fancy menu design.From there, it's two possibilities:-They care about the food at the expensive of design and it's going to be cheap and great (good) -They care about the food as much as the design and it's going to suck (bad)This switch happened after a new place opened in my neighborhood that had AI signage and is now my favorite lunch spot.
sentences.zh: 我曾经想“哇，他们根本不在乎”，就像我之前看 MS Word 中清晰制作的菜单一样：这个地方可能很便宜，而且他们不关心花哨的菜单设计。从那里，有两种可能性：-他们关心设计昂贵的食物，它会便宜而且很棒（好）-他们关心食物和设计一样多，但它会很糟糕（坏）这种转变发生在我的社区开了一家有人工智能的新地方之后标牌，现在是我最喜欢的午餐地点。

blocks.html_en:
Possibly, but I think it’s a bit more intuitive than that. It’s basically just like companies with terrible websites:

Most of them are terrible because they no longer exist, have no budget because they’re not making money, or because they’re bad at doing things generally.

But then there’s a set of companies that do fantastically well and are so busy serving customers (or are satisfied with their results) that their website doesn’t matter. In that case, the bad website is an indicator for a good product.

sentences.en: Possibly, but I think it’s a bit more intuitive than that.
sentences.zh: 有可能，但我认为这比这更直观。

sentences.en: It’s basically just like companies with terrible websites:Most of them are terrible because they no longer exist, have no budget because they’re not making money, or because they’re bad at doing things generally.But then there’s a set of companies that do fantastically well and are so busy serving customers (or are satisfied with their results) that their website doesn’t matter.
sentences.zh: 这基本上就像网站很糟糕的公司一样：它们中的大多数都很糟糕，因为它们不再存在，没有预算，因为它们不赚钱，或者因为它们一般不擅长做事。但也有一些公司做得非常好，并且忙于为客户服务（或对他们的结果感到满意），以至于他们的网站无关紧要。

sentences.en: In that case, the bad website is an indicator for a good product.
sentences.zh: 在这种情况下，糟糕的网站是好产品的指标。

blocks.html_en: I think its interesting how sensitive some people are to AI generated everything and then there's a group of people who just straight up don't care. No real middle ground.

sentences.zh: 我认为有趣的是，有些人对人工智能产生的一切非常敏感，但有一群人却根本不在乎。

sentences.en: No real middle ground.
sentences.zh: 没有真正的中间立场。

blocks.html_en: There's definitely a group of people who trumpet continually that AI is going to replace everything soon and anyone who doesn't use it maximally will be left behind.

blocks.html_en:
If 1000's of years from now aliens discovers these menus while excavating buried human cities they will get confused about what evolutionary dissonance was going on looking at the phenotypical structure of creatures in hand drawn paintings juxtaposed with AI drawn menus. "Did these humans who drew these live in different cut off worlds that these same animals evolved to look so different from each other?", "Did they engage in active genetic engineering instead(because they lived only for a few 1000 years in organized societies)?" they would hypothesize and get confused.

Future alien confusion is all that Gen-AI pictures add. There is no value to them that is remotely redeeming/useful to us today.

sentences.en: If 1000's of years from now aliens discovers these menus while excavating buried human cities they will get confused about what evolutionary dissonance was going on looking at the phenotypical structure of creatures in hand drawn paintings juxtaposed with AI drawn menus.
sentences.zh: 如果几千年后，外星人在挖掘埋藏的人类城市时发现了这些菜单，他们会对手绘绘画中生物的表型结构与人工智能绘制的菜单并列，对正在发生的进化失调感到困惑。

sentences.en: "Did these humans who drew these live in different cut off worlds that these same animals evolved to look so different from each other?", "Did they engage in active genetic engineering instead(because they lived only for a few 1000 years in organized societies)?"
sentences.zh: “画这些画的人类是否生活在不同的隔绝世界，以至于这些相同的动物进化得看起来彼此如此不同？”，“他们是否从事积极的基因工程（因为他们在有组织的社会中只生活了几千年）？”

sentences.en: they would hypothesize and get confused.Future alien confusion is all that Gen-AI pictures add.
sentences.zh: 他们会做出假设并感到困惑。未来的外星人困惑是 Gen-AI 图片所添加的全部内容。

sentences.en: There is no value to them that is remotely redeeming/useful to us today.
sentences.zh: 对于今天的我们来说，它们没有任何远程救赎/有用的价值。

sentences.zh: “画这些画的人类是否生活在不同的隔绝世界，以至于这些相同的动物进化得看起来彼此如此不同？”，“他们是否从事积极的基因工程（因为他们在有组织的社会中只生活了几千年）？”

sentences.en: they would hypothesize and get confused.Future alien confusion is all that Gen-AI pictures add.
sentences.zh: 他们会做出假设并感到困惑。未来的外星人困惑是 Gen-AI 图片所添加的全部内容。

sentences.en: There is no value to them that is remotely redeeming/useful to us today.
sentences.zh: 对于今天的我们来说，它们没有任何远程救赎/有用的价值。

blocks.html_en: If 1000's of years from now aliens discovers these menus while excavating buried human cities they will get confused about what evolutionary dissonance was going on looking at the phenotypical structure of creatures in hand drawn paintings juxtaposed with AI drawn menus. "Did these humans who drew these live in different cut off worlds that these same animals evolved to look so different from each other?", "Did they engage in active genetic engineering instead(because they lived only for a few 1000 years in organized societies)?" they would hypothesize and get confused.

blocks.html_en: You might enjoy “Motel of the Mysteries” then, a large format illustrated paperback that I grew up with, which showed some hilarious conclusions about 1980s ‘culture’ by some future-millennia archaeologists. They conclude for instance that the TV box is an object of worship because all the architecture in the motel is designed around or points toward it!

sentences.en: You might enjoy “Motel of the Mysteries” then, a large format illustrated paperback that I grew up with, which showed some hilarious conclusions about 1980s ‘culture’ by some future-millennia archaeologists.
sentences.zh: 那么你可能会喜欢《神秘汽车旅馆》，这是一本伴随我长大的大幅面插图平装本，其中展示了一些未来千年考古学家关于 20 世纪 80 年代“文化”的一些搞笑结论。

sentences.en: They conclude for instance that the TV box is an object of worship because all the architecture in the motel is designed around or points toward it!
sentences.zh: 例如，他们得出结论，电视盒是崇拜的对象，因为汽车旅馆的所有建筑都是围绕它设计或指向它！

blocks.html_en: Restaurants used to have stock images everywhere from menus, to flyers, and posters. I don't remember much talk about it to the extent we have with AI generated images. I prefer to judge restaurants on the quality and taste of food, unless they're charging me a fine-dining rate for it.

sentences.en: Restaurants used to have stock images everywhere from menus, to flyers, and posters.
sentences.zh: 过去，餐馆里到处都有库存图片，从菜单到传单和海报。

sentences.en: I don't remember much talk about it to the extent we have with AI generated images.
sentences.zh: 我不记得有太多关于人工智能生成图像的讨论。

sentences.en: I prefer to judge restaurants on the quality and taste of food, unless they're charging me a fine-dining rate for it.
sentences.zh: 我更喜欢根据食物的质量和味道来评判餐馆，除非他们向我收取高额用餐费用。

blocks.html_en: Google maps needs a tool to create menu from best user submitted pics. Still use AI to clean up lighting, masking, layout etc, but there's enough data to cover most popular dishes. Feels like all the REAL useful data / pieces are there to solve the problem.

sentences.en: Google maps needs a tool to create menu from best user submitted pics.
sentences.zh: 谷歌地图需要一个工具来根据用户提交的最佳图片创建菜单。

sentences.en: Still use AI to clean up lighting, masking, layout etc, but there's enough data to cover most popular dishes.
sentences.zh: 仍然使用人工智能来清理灯光、遮蔽、布局等，但有足够的数据来涵盖最流行的菜肴。

sentences.en: Feels like all the REAL useful data / pieces are there to solve the problem.
sentences.zh: 感觉所有真正有用的数据/片段都可以解决问题。

blocks.html_en:
I think if you showed these before/after menus to people from 5 years ago, most would tell you it’s a massive improvement.

But now many of us have been trained to detect slop from miles away so it just comes across as cheap.

I often feel the same way when I glance at a project’s documentation and it’s long and full of markdown styles and emojis: “the author didn’t think about the users at all, they just wanted to get this over with quickly, so now I have to be super suspicious of everything I read here and it might end up being a huge waste of my time”. A few years ago I’d probably think “wow, emojis!”

sentences.en: I think if you showed these before/after menus to people from 5 years ago, most would tell you it’s a massive improvement.But now many of us have been trained to detect slop from miles away so it just comes across as cheap.I often feel the same way when I glance at a project’s documentation and it’s long and full of markdown styles and emojis: “the author didn’t think about the users at all, they just wanted to get this over with quickly, so now I have to be super suspicious of everything I read here and it might end up being a huge waste of my time”.
sentences.zh: 我想如果你向人们展示这些 5 年前的前后菜单，大多数人会告诉你这是一个巨大的改进。但现在我们中的许多人都接受过训练，可以从几英里外检测出斜坡，所以它看起来很便宜。当我浏览一个项目的文档时，我经常有同样的感觉，它又长又充满了 Markdown 样式和表情符号：“作者根本没有考虑用户，他们只是想快速完成这件事，所以现在我必须非常怀疑我在这里读到的所有内容，最终可能会浪费我的时间”。

sentences.en: A few years ago I’d probably think “wow, emojis!”
sentences.zh: 几年前，我可能会想“哇，表情符号！”

blocks.html_en:
> The worst part is that I frequently work with small businesses in Austin and know that this is from a place of complete ignorance rather than feeling a sense of ownership in art/design they did not do.

> Yes I also understand many of them have slim margins, but TBH I would rather read a whole menu in Comic Sans, Papyrus, or Curlz MT than this garbage.

I don’t think it matters what principled vs ignorant people think but rather how many of each kind there are. Put differently, does the average person care?

Would they prefer a text only menu or an AI slop one, when the budget for making either is basically 0$?

Who the average customer is also might change based on generation (younger people might be more averse to AI, or maybe not really and the ones who are averse are louder), and probably also how used to AI slop people get.

sentences.en: > The worst part is that I frequently work with small businesses in Austin and know that this is from a place of complete ignorance rather than feeling a sense of ownership in art/design they did not do.> Yes I also understand many of them have slim margins, but TBH I would rather read a whole menu in Comic Sans, Papyrus, or Curlz MT than this garbage.I don’t think it matters what principled vs ignorant people think but rather how many of each kind there are.
sentences.zh: > 最糟糕的是，我经常与奥斯汀的小企业合作，我知道这是来自于完全无知的地方，而不是对他们没有做过的艺术/设计有一种主人翁感。> 是的，我也知道他们中的许多人利润微薄，但说实话，我宁愿阅读 Comic Sans、Papyrus 或 Curlz MT 的整个菜单，也不愿阅读这些垃圾。我认为有原则的人和无知的人怎么想并不重要，重要的是每种类型有多少。

sentences.en: Put differently, does the average person care?Would they prefer a text only menu or an AI slop one, when the budget for making either is basically 0$?Who the average customer is also might change based on generation (younger people might be more averse to AI, or maybe not really and the ones who are averse are louder), and probably also how used to AI slop people get.
sentences.zh: 换句话说，普通人会关心吗？当制作预算基本为 0 美元时，他们会更喜欢纯文本菜单还是人工智能菜单？普通客户也可能会根据世代而变化（年轻人可能更厌恶人工智能，或者可能不是真的，而且厌恶的人声音更大），而且可能还包括人们如何习惯人工智能菜单。

blocks.html_en:
> The worst part is that I frequently work with small businesses in Austin and know that this is from a place of complete ignorance rather than feeling a sense of ownership in art/design they did not do.

> Yes I also understand many of them have slim margins, but TBH I would rather read a whole menu in Comic Sans, Papyrus, or Curlz MT than this garbage.

I don’t think it matters what principled vs ignorant people think but rather how many of each kind there are. Put differently, does the average person care?

Would they prefer a text only menu or an AI slop one, when the budget for making either is basically 0$?

Who the average customer is also might change based on generation (younger people might be more averse to AI, or maybe not really and the ones who are averse are louder), and probably also how used to AI slop people get.

blocks.html_en:
I buy rice from an online store that carries speciality Japanese varieties. The rice is very good.

But this year they replaced their previously obviously-English-as-second-language-but-informative descriptions with just total slop that say absolutely nothing at all about the rice you’re buying. Every description is just platitudes about how good the rice is.

So as in TFA we get people who seemingly can’t resist “pressing the button” and getting nice shiny results even if they aren’t, like, good. It’s nuts. FWIW I emailed the rice shop and politely asked them to revert to the old descriptions but received no reply.

sentences.en: I buy rice from an online store that carries speciality Japanese varieties.
sentences.zh: 我从一家销售日本特色品种的网上商店购买大米。

sentences.en: The rice is very good.But this year they replaced their previously obviously-English-as-second-language-but-informative descriptions with just total slop that say absolutely nothing at all about the rice you’re buying.
sentences.zh: 大米非常好。但今年，他们用完全没有任何关于您购买的大米的信息，取代了以前明显以英语为第二语言但内容丰富的描述。

sentences.en: Every description is just platitudes about how good the rice is.So as in TFA we get people who seemingly can’t resist “pressing the button” and getting nice shiny results even if they aren’t, like, good.
sentences.zh: 每一个描述都只是关于大米有多好的陈词滥调。所以就像在 TFA 中一样，我们看到人们似乎无法抗拒“按下按钮”并获得漂亮闪亮的结果，即使他们不是，比如，好。

sentences.en: It’s nuts.
sentences.zh: 太疯狂了。

sentences.en: FWIW I emailed the rice shop and politely asked them to revert to the old descriptions but received no reply.
sentences.zh: FWIW我给米店发了电子邮件，礼貌地要求他们恢复旧的描述，但没有收到回复。

blocks.html_en:
We have RUINED the food industry. It started with gig-work delivery. Restaurants will never recover from GrubHub and ghost kitchens. Food quality and service died years ago.

Now, they don't have to pay for or manage delivery people, or pay anyone to make their menus for them.

There are no consequences to lying. People have a bad experience sure, but they'll forget next time they order off grubhub and simply search for "burrito near me" and click on the first good looking food.

sentences.en: We have RUINED the food industry.
sentences.zh: 我们毁了食品工业。

sentences.en: It started with gig-work delivery.
sentences.zh: 它始于零工交付。

sentences.en: Restaurants will never recover from GrubHub and ghost kitchens.
sentences.zh: 餐厅永远无法从 GrubHub 和幽灵厨房中恢复过来。

sentences.en: Food quality and service died years ago.Now, they don't have to pay for or manage delivery people, or pay anyone to make their menus for them.There are no consequences to lying.
sentences.zh: 食品质量和服务几年前就已经消失了。现在，他们不必支付或管理送货员的费用，也不必付钱给任何人为他们制作菜单。撒谎不会有任何后果。

sentences.en: People have a bad experience sure, but they'll forget next time they order off grubhub and simply search for "burrito near me" and click on the first good looking food.
sentences.zh: 人们肯定有过糟糕的经历，但下次他们从 grubhub 点餐时就会忘记，只需搜索“我附近的墨西哥卷饼”，然后点击第一个看起来不错的食物。

blocks.html_en:
Ironic that this would be physical spam and the complaint is AI.

Sure, it is is an environmental disaster, churned out in industrial quantities, low-effort, an emulsified layer of mediocrity, slop everywhere, fouls the air for miles around; but that's true of AI, too.

sentences.en: Ironic that this would be physical spam and the complaint is AI.Sure, it is is an environmental disaster, churned out in industrial quantities, low-effort, an emulsified layer of mediocrity, slop everywhere, fouls the air for miles around; but that's true of AI, too.
sentences.zh: 具有讽刺意味的是，这将是物理垃圾邮件，而投诉的是人工智能。当然，这是一场环境灾难，以工业量大量生产，不费力气，一层平庸的乳化层，到处都是，污染了方圆数英里的空气；但人工智能也是如此。

blocks.html_en:
Ok, I'll say it. I like the new menu better.

I do not understand the author's aversion. Sure we can talk about "picture of food" vs "what was served" but have you been... anywhere? This is the reality of all food advertisement. I've never in my life been served a plate of food that perfectly matched the picture unless it was a user-uploaded image. Getting a decent idea of what the dish looks like is valuable (especially for unfamiliar cuisine, obviously not unfamiliar to the author but I'd wager unfamiliar to most patrons).

Being anti-LLM/AI for the sake of it is boring. Show me mistakes on the menu or a level of unreality higher than the food industry at large and I'll care but I'm not seeing the problem here.

sentences.en: Ok, I'll say it.
sentences.zh: 好吧，我就说吧。

sentences.en: I like the new menu better.I do not understand the author's aversion.
sentences.zh: 我更喜欢新菜单。我不明白作者的反感。

sentences.en: Sure we can talk about "picture of food" vs "what was served" but have you been...
sentences.zh: 当然，我们可以讨论“食物的图片”与“提供的食物”，但是您...

sentences.en: anywhere?
sentences.zh: 任何地方？

sentences.en: This is the reality of all food advertisement.
sentences.zh: 这是所有食品广告的现实。

sentences.en: I've never in my life been served a plate of food that perfectly matched the picture unless it was a user-uploaded image.
sentences.zh: 我一生中从未吃过一盘与图片完美匹配的食物，除非它是用户上传的图像。

sentences.en: Getting a decent idea of what the dish looks like is valuable (especially for unfamiliar cuisine, obviously not unfamiliar to the author but I'd wager unfamiliar to most patrons).Being anti-LLM/AI for the sake of it is boring.
sentences.zh: 对这道菜的外观有一个不错的了解是很有价值的（特别是对于不熟悉的菜肴，显然对作者来说并不陌生，但我敢打赌对大多数顾客来说都不熟悉）。为此而反对 LLM/AI 很无聊。

sentences.en: Show me mistakes on the menu or a level of unreality higher than the food industry at large and I'll care but I'm not seeing the problem here.
sentences.zh: 如果我看到菜单上的错误或不真实程度高于整个食品行业，我会关心，但我在这里没有看到问题。

sentences.zh: 当然，我们可以讨论“食物的图片”与“提供的食物”，但是您...

sentences.en: anywhere?
sentences.zh: 任何地方？

sentences.en: This is the reality of all food advertisement.
sentences.zh: 这是所有食品广告的现实。

sentences.en: I've never in my life been served a plate of food that perfectly matched the picture unless it was a user-uploaded image.
sentences.zh: 我一生中从未吃过一盘与图片完美匹配的食物，除非它是用户上传的图像。

sentences.en: Getting a decent idea of what the dish looks like is valuable (especially for unfamiliar cuisine, obviously not unfamiliar to the author but I'd wager unfamiliar to most patrons).
sentences.zh: 了解这道菜的样子是很有价值的（特别是对于不熟悉的菜肴，显然对作者来说并不陌生，但我敢打赌对大多数顾客来说都不熟悉）。

blocks.html_en: Being anti-LLM/AI for the sake of it is boring. Show me mistakes on the menu or a level of unreality higher than the food industry at large and I'll care but I'm not seeing the problem here.

sentences.en: Being anti-LLM/AI for the sake of it is boring.
sentences.zh: 为了它而反对 LLM/AI 是很无聊的。

sentences.en: Show me mistakes on the menu or a level of unreality higher than the food industry at large and I'll care but I'm not seeing the problem here.
sentences.zh: 如果我看到菜单上的错误或不真实程度高于整个食品行业，我会关心，但我在这里没有看到问题。

blocks.html_en:
Everything looks brown, it's crammed together, those not-photos make it feel really cramped in the third column, it looks like every other AI-generated menu and it's lost the clarity the old menu had.

And yes, there's a very long history of food places using unrealistic images of someone else's version of the food on the menus. That doesn't mean that it's okay to now use LLM versions of the food, because I never thought it was okay to use those stylised photos either.

sentences.en: Everything looks brown, it's crammed together, those not-photos make it feel really cramped in the third column, it looks like every other AI-generated menu and it's lost the clarity the old menu had.And yes, there's a very long history of food places using unrealistic images of someone else's version of the food on the menus.
sentences.zh: 所有东西看起来都是棕色的，都挤在一起，那些不是照片的东西让它在第三栏中感觉非常局促，它看起来就像所有其他人工智能生成的菜单一样，而且失去了旧菜单的清晰度。是的，有很长的历史，美食场所在菜单上使用别人版本的食物的不切实际的图像。

sentences.en: That doesn't mean that it's okay to now use LLM versions of the food, because I never thought it was okay to use those stylised photos either.
sentences.zh: 这并不意味着现在可以使用法学硕士版本的食物，因为我也从未想过使用那些风格化的照片是可以的。

You can get damn close in Japan. I've even had situations where the food looks _better_ than the photos.

blocks.html_en:
I feel like we read different articles, because without context, the pictured dish looked like overcooked pork chops served next to a type of carbonara tagliatelle topped with a fried egg & chives. I can't imagine anyone has ever set out to make spamsilog and ended up with the generated dish.

It's different in kind to a "my Big Mac is more squashed than the advert on TV" type situation.

blocks.html_en: It'd be one thing if it was just the problem of a generic AI slop graphic design that all looks the same, the problem is when it generates pictures of food that literally looks disgusting. I've seen some of these posted online where the food literally looks like it's infected (fries with weird black splotches), or the jalapeños look like tires, and it all triggers trypophobia.

sentences.en: It'd be one thing if it was just the problem of a generic AI slop graphic design that all looks the same, the problem is when it generates pictures of food that literally looks disgusting.
sentences.zh: 如果这只是一个看起来都一样的通用人工智能倾斜图形设计的问题，那就是一回事了，问题是当它生成的食物图片看起来确实很恶心时。

sentences.en: I've seen some of these posted online where the food literally looks like it's infected (fries with weird black splotches), or the jalapeños look like tires, and it all triggers trypophobia.

blocks.html_en:
I’m ambivalent towards AI generated poster or other business design. It might leave a bad taste in my mouth but it’s whatever. While I’m against it, I can understand why for many businesses it’s an easy and convenient choice.

What makes me feel almost physical disgust is the food in AI generated posters or menus. There’s something deeply uncannily unappetizing about the food. And once I can identify it as AI generated, it’s even worse… there’s something almost creepy about knowing the image is a computers best approximation of something humans want to ingest.

I cannot understand how a food establishment that takes themselves seriously would want to use generative assets like this… can’t they see how unappetizing their own food looks??

sentences.en: I’m ambivalent towards AI generated poster or other business design.
sentences.zh: 我对人工智能生成的海报或其他商业设计持矛盾态度。

sentences.en: It might leave a bad taste in my mouth but it’s whatever.
sentences.zh: 它可能会在我嘴里留下不好的味道，但无论如何。

sentences.en: While I’m against it, I can understand why for many businesses it’s an easy and convenient choice.What makes me feel almost physical disgust is the food in AI generated posters or menus.
sentences.zh: 虽然我反对，但我可以理解为什么对于许多企业来说这是一个简单方便的选择。让我感到几乎身体厌恶的是人工智能生成的海报或菜单中的食物。

sentences.en: There’s something deeply uncannily unappetizing about the food.
sentences.zh: 食物有一种非常令人反胃的味道。

sentences.en: And once I can identify it as AI generated, it’s even worse…
sentences.zh: 一旦我能识别出它是人工智能生成的，那就更糟糕了……

sentences.en: there’s something almost creepy about knowing the image is a computers best approximation of something humans want to ingest.I cannot understand how a food establishment that takes themselves seriously would want to use generative assets like this…
sentences.zh: 知道这个图像是计算机对人类想要摄取的东西的最佳近似，这有点令人毛骨悚然。我无法理解一个认真对待自己的食品企业如何想要使用这样的生成资产......

sentences.en: can’t they see how unappetizing their own food looks??
sentences.zh: 难道他们看不到自己的食物看起来有多难吃吗？

blocks.html_en: AI menus and signage are quickly becoming the dog whistle for "cheap". It's telling you what kind of quality and service you should expect in the rest of their product or service. Pretty soon people will catch on, and start avoiding these places. Once that happens, generative AI image models will be hard-pressed to drop the association. It's like walking into a dollar store -- everything can be made cheaply, so it doesn't matter if something there is actually of any value.

sentences.en: AI menus and signage are quickly becoming the dog whistle for "cheap".
sentences.zh: 人工智能菜单和标牌正迅速成为“便宜”的代名词。

sentences.en: It's telling you what kind of quality and service you should expect in the rest of their product or service.
sentences.zh: 它告诉您您应该期望他们的其他产品或服务具有什么样的质量和服务。

sentences.en: Pretty soon people will catch on, and start avoiding these places.
sentences.zh: 很快人们就会明白，并开始避开这些地方。

sentences.en: Once that happens, generative AI image models will be hard-pressed to drop the association.
sentences.zh: 一旦发生这种情况，生成式人工智能图像模型将很难摆脱这种关联。

sentences.en: It's like walking into a dollar store -- everything can be made cheaply, so it doesn't matter if something there is actually of any value.
sentences.zh: 这就像走进一家一元店——所有东西都可以廉价制造，所以里面的东西是否真的有价值并不重要。

blocks.html_en: AI menus and signage are quickly becoming the dog whistle for "cheap". It's telling you what kind of quality and service you should expect in the rest of their product or service. Pretty soon people will catch on, and start avoiding these places. Once that happens, generative AI image models will be hard-pressed to drop the association. It's like walking into a dollar store -- everything can be made cheaply, so it doesn't matter if something there is actually of any value.

sentences.en: AI menus and signage are quickly becoming the dog whistle for "cheap".
sentences.zh: 人工智能菜单和标牌正迅速成为“便宜”的代名词。

sentences.en: It's telling you what kind of quality and service you should expect in the rest of their product or service.
sentences.zh: 它告诉您您应该期望他们的其他产品或服务具有什么样的质量和服务。

sentences.en: Pretty soon people will catch on, and start avoiding these places.
sentences.zh: 很快人们就会明白，并开始避开这些地方。

sentences.en: Once that happens, generative AI image models will be hard-pressed to drop the association.
sentences.zh: 一旦发生这种情况，生成式人工智能图像模型将很难摆脱这种关联。

sentences.en: It's like walking into a dollar store -- everything can be made cheaply, so it doesn't matter if something there is actually of any value.
sentences.zh: 这就像走进一家一元店——所有东西都可以廉价制造，所以里面的东西是否真的有价值并不重要。

blocks.html_en: Except sometimes cheap places are great because the food is good and they don't spend money on ambience and other pretentious expenditures.

sentences.zh: 除了有时便宜的地方很棒，因为食物很好，而且他们不会在氛围和其他自命不凡的支出上花钱。

blocks.html_en: Honestly pictures in restaurant menus have been pretty bad long before AI. I don't understand how it can be accepted to show a picture that looks nothing like the served food.

blocks.html_en:
It doesn't seem to be as much a trend in the UK - fast food, takeaways etc have some leaking in of AI - but when I went to Korea I was blown away by how many restaurants had these AI style menus.

It's a shame because it takes away the choice of either seeing the real product, or imagining it - I'm left with this shitty generated impression of what the food won't look like.

sentences.en: It doesn't seem to be as much a trend in the UK - fast food, takeaways etc have some leaking in of AI - but when I went to Korea I was blown away by how many restaurants had these AI style menus.It's a shame because it takes away the choice of either seeing the real product, or imagining it - I'm left with this shitty generated impression of what the food won't look like.
sentences.zh: 这在英国似乎并没有成为一种趋势——快餐、外卖等都有一些人工智能的渗透——但当我去韩国时，我对有多少餐馆拥有这些人工智能风格的菜单感到震惊。这是一种耻辱，因为它剥夺了我要么看到真实产品，要么想象它的选择——我留下了这种食物看起来不像的糟糕印象。

blocks.html_en: IDK, the menu is not that bad for a person that is outside the context and sees both menus for the first time. The photo is also not that bad -- of course it would be better to use real photos, I've no idea why they aren't doing this (probably reprinting menus is not a trivial problem), but it kind of gives some hints about the dish. As someone who doesn't have a clue, the new menu gives me some information that the old menu didn't.

blocks.html_en:
Anti-AI derangement syndrome is real. Yes it’s not a great looking menu design, but most people (over in Europe at least) have no idea about phillipino food, so some idea what sisig or adobo is great. Anger over restaurant owner having access and capability to this insanely powerful tool is just so mean.

Do you also hate if they use microwave, cooked on induction, used wrong type of oil, antibiotic meat, pesticidal vags, chemical cleaners, unethical coffee and people who work there are wrong race/gender/sexuality?

sentences.en: Anti-AI derangement syndrome is real.
sentences.zh: 抗人工智能紊乱综合症是真实存在的。

sentences.en: Yes it’s not a great looking menu design, but most people (over in Europe at least) have no idea about phillipino food, so some idea what sisig or adobo is great.

sentences.en: Anger over restaurant owner having access and capability to this insanely powerful tool is just so mean.Do you also hate if they use microwave, cooked on induction, used wrong type of oil, antibiotic meat, pesticidal vags, chemical cleaners, unethical coffee and people who work there are wrong race/gender/sexuality?
sentences.zh: 对餐厅老板能够使用这种极其强大的工具的愤怒是如此卑鄙。您是否也讨厌他们使用微波炉、电磁炉烹饪、使用错误类型的油、抗生素肉、杀虫剂瓶、化学清洁剂、不道德的咖啡以及在那里工作的人的种族/性别/性取向错误？

I wanted to be interested by the point of the author of this blog post, but a detail got my intention and then couldn't stop thinking about it:

Why TF did he blur all the prices of the menu in the pictures?

blocks.html_en:
Outside of cheap takeout places, I neither need nor want to see pictures of food on menus, because I can read the menu and decide for myself whether I want to try something based off the description. I'd rather a blown out flash photo of a plate of noodles than a generated image though.

I was under the impression that FTC laws require food advertisement photos to be the "real" thing. I don't know if the FTC covers food service but if so I wonder if they just don't want to bother slapping restaurants with fines over generated product images in menus (or if menus just aren't covered by the same regulation). Also I wonder if there's some grey area when it pertains to "photos" vs "generated images" (though the latter is clearly intended to stand in for the former).

sentences.en: Outside of cheap takeout places, I neither need nor want to see pictures of food on menus, because I can read the menu and decide for myself whether I want to try something based off the description.
sentences.zh: 除了便宜的外卖店之外，我既不需要也不想在菜单上看到食物的图片，因为我可以阅读菜单并根据描述自己决定是否要尝试一些东西。

sentences.en: I'd rather a blown out flash photo of a plate of noodles than a generated image though.I was under the impression that FTC laws require food advertisement photos to be the "real" thing.
sentences.zh: 不过，我更喜欢一盘面条的闪光照片，而不是生成的图像。我的印象是，联邦贸易委员会法律要求食品广告照片是“真实”的东西。

sentences.en: I don't know if the FTC covers food service but if so I wonder if they just don't want to bother slapping restaurants with fines over generated product images in menus (or if menus just aren't covered by the same regulation).
sentences.zh: 我不知道联邦贸易委员会是否涵盖餐饮服务，但如果是的话，我想知道他们是否只是不想对餐厅对菜单中生成的产品图像进行罚款（或者如果菜单不属于同一法规）。

sentences.en: Also I wonder if there's some grey area when it pertains to "photos" vs "generated images" (though the latter is clearly intended to stand in for the former).
sentences.zh: 我还想知道当涉及“照片”与“生成的图像”时是否存在一些灰色区域（尽管后者显然是为了代替前者）。

blocks.html_en:
Outside of cheap takeout places, I neither need nor want to see pictures of food on menus, because I can read the menu and decide for myself whether I want to try something based off the description. I'd rather a blown out flash photo of a plate of noodles than a generated image though.

I was under the impression that FTC laws require food advertisement photos to be the "real" thing. I don't know if the FTC covers food service but if so I wonder if they just don't want to bother slapping restaurants with fines over generated product images in menus (or if menus just aren't covered by the same regulation). Also I wonder if there's some grey area when it pertains to "photos" vs "generated images" (though the latter is clearly intended to stand in for the former).

sentences.en: Outside of cheap takeout places, I neither need nor want to see pictures of food on menus, because I can read the menu and decide for myself whether I want to try something based off the description.
sentences.zh: 除了便宜的外卖店之外，我既不需要也不想在菜单上看到食物的图片，因为我可以阅读菜单并根据描述自己决定是否要尝试一些东西。

sentences.en: I'd rather a blown out flash photo of a plate of noodles than a generated image though.I was under the impression that FTC laws require food advertisement photos to be the "real" thing.
sentences.zh: 不过，我更喜欢一盘面条的闪光照片，而不是生成的图像。我的印象是，联邦贸易委员会法律要求食品广告照片是“真实”的东西。

sentences.en: I don't know if the FTC covers food service but if so I wonder if they just don't want to bother slapping restaurants with fines over generated product images in menus (or if menus just aren't covered by the same regulation).
sentences.zh: 我不知道联邦贸易委员会是否涵盖餐饮服务，但如果是的话，我想知道他们是否只是不想对餐厅对菜单中生成的产品图像进行罚款（或者如果菜单不属于同一法规）。

sentences.en: Also I wonder if there's some grey area when it pertains to "photos" vs "generated images" (though the latter is clearly intended to stand in for the former).
sentences.zh: 我还想知道当涉及“照片”与“生成的图像”时是否存在一些灰色区域（尽管后者显然是为了代替前者）。

blocks.html_en: There is this new thing where people love to show off their sophistication that they can "spot AI". Every time they see an a Claudeism, every time they see an emdash, they talk about how they feel sick to their stomach and are ready to boycott the business that may or may not be guilty of the high crime of using a computer tool.

sentences.en: There is this new thing where people love to show off their sophistication that they can "spot AI".
sentences.zh: 有一种新事物，人们喜欢炫耀他们可以“发现人工智能”的复杂性。

sentences.en: Every time they see an a Claudeism, every time they see an emdash, they talk about how they feel sick to their stomach and are ready to boycott the business that may or may not be guilty of the high crime of using a computer tool.
sentences.zh: 每当他们看到克劳德主义，每次看到emdash，他们都会谈论他们如何感到恶心，并准备抵制可能或可能不犯有使用计算机工具的高额犯罪的企业。

blocks.html_en: Yeah, this thought is lame. It's not so bad in other areas, but in food, I would really like to see REAL pictures of what I'm about to order. I mean, "fake" or deceitful pictures always existed, but at least there were real ingredients and so on. I will NEVER boycott some family restaurant because the menu is AI generated, but it is tasteless and I prefer not to see it.

content_html_zh: 人格的丧失是真实的。 我见过的最令人心碎的事情是在学校或任何有孩子参与的地方。例如，在我当地的幼儿园，墙上有一张传单，上面写着关于游泳池要带什么东西的信息。 它有我见过的最粗糙的鱼、螃蟹和海马。 很明显，无论是谁画的，都没有任何与铅笔相似的东西的经验，而且从任何标准来看，人们都不会将这些画定义为“好”。 也许是看门人画的，或者老师画的时间太少了？ 不管怎样，我再怎么强调也不为过，我非常喜欢它，而不是他们提出的新的人工智能生成的，如果他们在提示中输入“海洋生物”，就会得到通用的人工智能生成的海洋生物。 我自己也无法告诉你为什么，如果你并排看两个版本，人工智能版本无疑要好得多。 但... 只是感觉不对。 离开。 不合适。 我知道我听起来像个勒德分子，但看起来不太对劲。 这太不可思议了，我实在无法解释其中的原因。 这就像在一部可爱的儿童卡通片中看到一个完美绘制的、逼真的、解剖学上准确的人类。 它只是不适合其他一切。 这是一所幼儿园，里面应该充满了“糟糕”的图画，你知道吗？别让我开始谈论他们张贴人工智能过滤的集体照片的时间。 我几乎崩溃了，而且我（大部分）是人工智能专业人士。原因是人工智能艺术现在已经商品化。例如，当主人有空闲时间休闲时，美容标准会直接跟踪身体的外观。 例如，在某些文化中，晒黑的皮肤过去是丑陋的，而苍白的皮肤更美丽，因为晒黑的皮肤意味着你在阳光下工作，而苍白的皮肤意味着你可以在室内，从事更高地位的工作或不工作。 现在，苍白意味着困在室内工作，晒黑意味着外出休闲。 这种情况突然发生了变化，因为“健康”意味着你有时间去做这件事。在我们消费的媒体和艺术中，所涉及的人类努力的数量决定了我们下意识地重视它的程度。 例如，人类参与写作的微妙迹象会让我们更喜欢写作。 由真人制作的清晰视频会更有趣。 就像一些对艺术充满热情的人在家做的饭菜总是比连锁餐厅的饭菜味道更好，即使餐厅的饭菜在技术上更复杂或者使用更好的食材。随着人工智能越来越多地接管，它的直接输出和闻起来像直接输出的东西将越来越不受欢迎。对于真正了解这一点的人来说，商业中有大量的市场机会。> 我见过的最令人心碎的地方是在学校或任何有孩子参与的地方。并不令人心碎，但最让我兴奋的是来自工艺品博览会和更糟糕的地方的传单，承诺教授工艺品/艺术。 这怎么不是一个要快速朝另一个方向走的标志呢？ 或者它可能是故意的，就像一些不良垃圾邮件文本显然是故意清除那些不太可能陷入骗局的人......我和我的妻子只是周末不去看工艺品展（她有写科幻书的爱好，我们去街头集会卖书）。 至少有两家供应商出售“手工制作”的绘画，我可以看出，这些绘画是人工智能生成的图片粘贴到一块木头上，然后涂上几层厚厚的透明涂层，使其具有画笔效果。 此外，他们的横幅是人工智能生成的。 不诚实得令人厌恶。当海报从手绘变成电脑制作时，感觉很相似。 个性从手写字体或古怪的动态创建字体到 Comic Sans。 对我来说，这感觉像是一个新的水平。说实话，大多数菜单对我来说看起来就像是人工智能出现之前的菜单……我知道这一点，而且在像幼儿园这样本质上充满灵魂的环境中，这一点变得极其明显。尽管如此，虽然“这是人工智能生成的垃圾”及其变体本身都是完全有效的理由，但对于普通人来说，它们太肤浅了，因为他们看了这两个版本，更喜欢人工智能版本，因为它看起来“更好”。 我可能会听起来很疯狂和/或自命不凡地试图向他解释为什么我更喜欢画得很糟糕的作品而不是完美的人工智能生成的作品，我自己也不知道到底为什么。另外，为什么里面有海洋生物，当然那不是应该在游泳池里的东西。就像偶尔在有鱼的池子里游泳的人一样。 这可能是真的。 （谷歌悉尼海洋泳池看看我在说什么）这帮助了我对那些迂腐的老派 HN 评论的怀旧渴望，现在我似乎很少看到这些评论了……但坦率地说，小时候在游泳池里玩耍的时候经常围绕着假装成某种海洋生物。显而易见的答案是因为那个古老的、粗略绘制的标志确实如此（要更深入地了解，您必须询问绘制它的看门人。） 现在它已经完全脱离了它的起源并且毫无意义。这个问题可以回答的唯一原因是因为发布者看到了旧标志。 当所有看到旧标志的人都离开后，他们就会无缘无故地继续这样做。 当有人问起这件事时，一些怪人就会喋喋不休地谈论切斯特顿的栅栏。如果你不喜欢它，你可以和做这件事的人谈谈，并告诉他们“使用人工智能来做这件事是可以的，只是不要满足于你的初稿！”我认为人工智能图片有这种没有灵魂的氛围，因为它们是弗兰肯斯坦的创作：许多其他图片以马虎的方式缝合在一起。 一幅人造的图片，即使是一张糟糕的图片，也是通过勾勒出一个想法和存在而制成的，如果形象地说，图片背后的这个想法赋予了它灵魂。这是生活各个方面的公司化。 这是一个非常糟糕的问题，以至于许多人甚至在自己的事情上自愿采用中性的企业品牌风格。 听听人们是如何交谈的：过去，当人们在个人场合使用“协同”这个词时，我们会嘲笑他们并辱骂他们，而现在这个词的使用却带有讽刺意味。人类是社会性动物。 我们渴望事物具有个人风格。 这就是为什么我们将小商店理想化。 但我们也规避风险。 在大型连锁餐厅吃饭比在当地餐馆吃饭更容易。看看乡村音乐产业就知道了。 它实际上是一条“小镇怀旧”污水的装配线。 然而，小城镇最容易叛逃到沃尔玛或达乐百货。人们发布了多产的艺术家，称之为人工智能，但在大揭露之前，每个人都讨厌他们。 坦白说，这可能就在你的脑海里。这些“大家”是谁？ 我怀疑你实际上指的是“许多随机的社交媒体用户”，这只能证明从普通社交媒体中获得负面反应是微不足道的；不完全是一个突破性的发现。有时是，有时不是。如果不考虑客观质量，《外太空9号计划》和《氩之眼》就会受到高度评价，而不是笑话和饮酒游戏的笑柄。这些都是臭名昭著的例子，他们有幸被嘲笑，还有更多的例子只是因为他们的无能而逐渐被遗忘。我在有限的时间内看过各种各样的菜单。 这基本上就像网站很糟糕的公司一样：它们中的大多数都很糟糕，因为它们不再存在，没有预算，因为它们不赚钱，或者因为它们一般不擅长做事。但也有一些公司做得非常好，并且忙于为客户服务（或对他们的结果感到满意），以至于他们的网站无关紧要。 在这种情况下，糟糕的网站是好产品的指标。我认为有趣的是，有些人对人工智能产生的一切非常敏感，但有一群人却根本不在乎。 没有真正的中间立场。肯定有一群人不断鼓吹人工智能将很快取代一切，任何不充分利用它的人都会被抛在后面。如果几千年后，外星人在挖掘埋藏的人类城市时发现了这些菜单，他们会对手绘绘画中生物的表型结构与人工智能绘制的菜单并列，对正在发生的进化失调感到困惑。 “画这些画的人类是否生活在不同的隔绝世界，以至于这些相同的动物进化得看起来彼此如此不同？”，“他们是否从事积极的基因工程（因为他们在有组织的社会中只生活了几千年）？” 他们会做出假设并感到困惑。未来的外星人困惑是 Gen-AI 图片所添加的全部内容。 我更喜欢根据食物的质量和味道来评判餐馆，除非他们向我收取高额用餐费用。然后你意识到下一个版本是没​​有人类的菜单，只有人工智能的菜单，而菜单本身就是人工智能。谷歌地图需要一个工具来根据用户提交的最佳图片创建菜单。 仍然使用人工智能来清理灯光、遮蔽、布局等，但有足够的数据来涵盖最流行的菜肴。 感觉所有真正有用的数据/片段都可以解决问题。我想如果你向人们展示这些 5 年前的前后菜单，大多数人会告诉你这是一个巨大的改进。但现在我们中的许多人都接受过训练，可以从几英里外检测出斜坡，所以它看起来很便宜。当我浏览一个项目的文档时，我经常有同样的感觉，它又长又充满了 Markdown 样式和表情符号：“作者根本没有考虑用户，他们只是想快速完成这件事，所以现在我必须非常怀疑我在这里读到的所有内容，最终可能会浪费我的时间”。 几年前，我可能会想“哇，表情符号！”不知何故，在荷兰，我们似乎基本上设法逃脱了这一点。 但这只是因为人们之前并没有真正在菜单上放任何装饰，而且没有装饰仍然比狗屎AI装饰便宜。+ 设计看起来比“简约”风格的原创更好 - 左上角的菜肴不在菜单上（为什么它在那里） - 为什么菜单上有一头营养不良的牛？++ 产品的照片，这样你就可以看到你会得到什么 - 照片与实际餐点不符> 最糟糕的是，我经常与奥斯汀的小企业合作，我知道这是来自于完全无知的地方，而不是对他们没有做过的艺术/设计有一种主人翁感。> 是的，我也知道他们中的许多人利润微薄，但说实话，我宁愿阅读 Comic Sans、Papyrus 或 Curlz MT 的整个菜单，也不愿阅读这些垃圾。我认为有原则的人和无知的人怎么想并不重要，重要的是每种类型有多少。 换句话说，普通人会关心吗？当制作预算基本为 0 美元时，他们会更喜欢纯文本菜单还是人工智能菜单？普通客户也可能会根据世代而变化（年轻人可能更厌恶人工智能，或者可能不是真的，而且厌恶的人声音更大），而且可能还包括人们如何习惯人工智能菜单。我从一家销售日本特色品种的网上商店购买大米。 大米非常好。但今年，他们用完全没有任何关于您购买的大米的信息，取代了以前明显以英语为第二语言但内容丰富的描述。 每一个描述都只是关于大米有多好的陈词滥调。所以就像在 TFA 中一样，我们看到人们似乎无法抗拒“按下按钮”并获得漂亮闪亮的结果，即使他们不是，比如，好。 太疯狂了。 FWIW我给米店发了电子邮件，礼貌地要求他们恢复旧的描述，但没有收到回复。我们毁了食品工业。 它始于零工交付。 餐厅永远无法从 GrubHub 和幽灵厨房中恢复过来。 食品质量和服务几年前就已经消失了。现在，他们不必支付或管理送货员的费用，也不必付钱给任何人为他们制作菜单。撒谎不会有任何后果。 人们肯定有过糟糕的经历，但下次他们从 grubhub 点餐时就会忘记，只需搜索“我附近的墨西哥卷饼”，然后点击第一个看起来不错的食物。具有讽刺意味的是，这将是物理垃圾邮件，而投诉的是人工智能。当然，这是一场环境灾难，以工业量大量生产，不费力气，一层平庸的乳化层，到处都是，污染了方圆数英里的空气；但人工智能也是如此。好吧，我就说吧。 我更喜欢新菜单。我不明白作者的反感。 当然，我们可以讨论“食物的图片”与“提供的食物”，但是您... 任何地方？ 这是所有食品广告的现实。 我一生中从未吃过一盘与图片完美匹配的食物，除非它是用户上传的图像。 对这道菜的外观有一个不错的了解是很有价值的（特别是对于不熟悉的菜肴，显然对作者来说并不陌生，但我敢打赌对大多数顾客来说都不熟悉）。为此而反对 LLM/AI 很无聊。 如果我看到菜单上的错误或不真实程度高于整个食品行业，我会关心，但我在这里没有看到问题。所有东西看起来都是棕色的，都挤在一起，那些不是照片的东西让它在第三栏中感觉非常局促，它看起来就像所有其他人工智能生成的菜单一样，而且失去了旧菜单的清晰度。是的，有很长的历史，美食场所在菜单上使用别人版本的食物的不切实际的图像。 这并不意味着现在可以使用法学硕士版本的食物，因为我也从未想过使用那些风格化的照片是可以的。> 我一生中从未吃过一盘与图片完美匹配的食物，除非它是用户上传的图片。在日本你可以非常接近。 我什至遇到过食物看起来比照片更好的情况。我觉得我们读了不同的文章，因为如果没有上下文，图中的菜看起来就像煮过头的猪排，旁边是一种上面放有煎鸡蛋和韭菜的意面干面条。 我无法想象有人曾经着手制作垃圾邮件并最终得到了生成的菜肴。这与“我的巨无霸比电视上的广告更压扁”类型的情况不同。如果这只是一个看起来都一样的通用人工智能倾斜图形设计的问题，那就是一回事了，问题是当它生成的食物图片看起来确实很恶心时。 我在网上看到过一些这样的帖子，其中的食物看起来像是被感染了（薯条上有奇怪的黑色斑点），或者墨西哥辣椒看起来像轮胎，这一切都会引发密集恐惧症。我对人工智能生成的海报或其他商业设计持矛盾态度。 它可能会在我嘴里留下不好的味道，但无论如何。 虽然我反对，但我可以理解为什么对于许多企业来说这是一个简单方便的选择。让我感到几乎身体厌恶的是人工智能生成的海报或菜单中的食物。 食物有一种非常令人反胃的味道。 一旦我能识别出它是人工智能生成的，那就更糟糕了…… 知道这个图像是计算机对人类想要摄取的东西的最佳近似，这有点令人毛骨悚然。我无法理解一个认真对待自己的食品企业如何想要使用这样的生成资产...... 难道他们看不到自己的食物看起来有多难吃吗？人工智能菜单和标牌正迅速成为“便宜”的代名词。 它告诉您您应该期望他们的其他产品或服务具有什么样的质量和服务。 很快人们就会明白，并开始避开这些地方。 一旦发生这种情况，生成式人工智能图像模型将很难摆脱这种关联。 这就像走进一家一元店——所有东西都可以廉价制造，所以里面的东西是否真的有价值并不重要。除了有时便宜的地方很棒，因为食物很好，而且他们不会在氛围和其他自命不凡的支出上花钱。老实说，早在人工智能出现之前，餐厅菜单上的图片就已经很糟糕了。 我不明白如何接受展示一张看起来与所提供的食物完全不同的图片。这在英国似乎并没有成为一种趋势——快餐、外卖等都有一些人工智能的渗透——但当我去韩国时，我对有多少餐馆拥有这些人工智能风格的菜单感到震惊。这是一种耻辱，因为它剥夺了我要么看到真实产品，要么想象它的选择——我留下了这种食物看起来不像的糟糕印象。IDK，对于一个在上下文之外并且第一次看到这两个菜单的人来说，菜单并没有那么糟糕。 照片也没有那么糟糕——当然，最好使用真实照片，我不知道他们为什么不这样做（可能重印菜单不是一个小问题），但它有点给出了有关这道菜的一些提示。 作为一个不知道的人，新菜单给了我一些旧菜单没有的信息。抗人工智能紊乱综合症是真实存在的。 是的，这不是一个好看的菜单设计，但大多数人（至少在欧洲）对菲律宾食物一无所知，所以有些人知道 sisig 或 adobo 是什么很棒。 对餐厅老板能够使用这种极其强大的工具的愤怒是如此卑鄙。您是否也讨厌他们使用微波炉、电磁炉烹饪、使用错误类型的油、抗生素肉、杀虫剂瓶、化学清洁剂、不道德的咖啡以及在那里工作的人的种族/性别/性取向错误？网站上也有很多这样的内容。 当年有一个设计相当不错，但最近我收到了不少要求将其更改为流行的线性风格 AI 设计的请求。 随着越来越多的网站使用人工智能生成的图像，我认为有些人已经将其视为趋势是的，完全正确。 Bootstrap 使布局同质化，现在人工智能似乎同时使布局和图像同质化。我本想对这篇博文的作者的观点感兴趣，但一个细节让我意犹未尽，然后忍不住想起来：为什么TF他要把图片中菜单的价格全部模糊掉？除了便宜的外卖店之外，我既不需要也不想在菜单上看到食物的图片，因为我可以阅读菜单并根据描述自己决定是否要尝试一些东西。 不过，我更喜欢一盘面条的闪光照片，而不是生成的图像。我的印象是，联邦贸易委员会法律要求食品广告照片是“真实”的东西。 我不知道联邦贸易委员会是否涵盖餐饮服务，但如果是的话，我想知道他们是否只是不想对餐厅对菜单中生成的产品图像进行罚款（或者如果菜单不属于同一法规）。 我还想知道当涉及“照片”与“生成的图像”时是否存在一些灰色区域（尽管后者显然是为了代替前者）。为什么不直接告诉他们的员工，他们的菜单让他们的食物看起来很恶心，他们应该使用旧的菜单呢？ 没有真正的经济损失，而且能够帮助小企业是件好事。 业主可能不太了解，或者有倾斜卷轴告诉他们这样做普通人真的觉得这些有吸引力吗？ 它们看起来就像 60 年代蝙蝠侠中的罗宾演员制作了一个看起来像恶意软件的狗粮品牌有一种新事物，人们喜欢炫耀他们可以“发现人工智能”的复杂性。 每当他们看到克劳德主义，每次看到emdash，他们都会谈论他们如何感到恶心，并准备抵制可能或可能不犯有使用计算机工具的高额犯罪的企业。是的，这个想法很蹩脚。 在其他方面还不错，但在食物方面，我真的很想看到我要点的食物的真实照片。 我的意思是，“假”或欺骗性的图片一直存在，但至少有真实的成分等等。 我永远不会抵制一些家庭餐馆，因为菜单是人工智能生成的，但它是无味的，我宁愿不看到它。
